1000686522.english: Please all maintain a safe distance.

1000686526.english: If you reach out your hand, I will gladly take it—
1000686526.korean: 당신이 손을 내밀면 얼마든지 그 손을 잡아드릴-

1000686529.english: This is Hero! Speaker Drone Destruction Detected!
1000686529.korean: 여기는 히어로! 스피커 드론 격파 탐지!

1000686532.english: Please do not attempt to capture them alive; instead, focus on disrupting them and signaling their location!
1000686532.korean: 무리하게 생포하지 말고 교란과 위치를 알리는 데에 집중해주세요!

1000686533.english: Shirayuri. Please send the real-time location of JangHwa and the hero.
1000686533.korean: 시라유리. 장화와 히어로의 위치를 실시간으로 전송해주세요.

1000686534.english: Mom! There are AGS in front!

1000686537.english: Shut up! Shut up! Shut up!

1000686538.english: Shut it! Don't pretend you understand me!

1000686539.english: Do you know who made me like this?!

1000686543.english: A brat obsessed with childish hero games?

1000686544.english: Then that makes you the evil villain who gets vanquished by the righteous hero?
1000686544.korean: 그러는 너는 정의로운 영웅에게 토벌되는 사악한 악당이고?

1000686545.english: And I think I'm taller than you, too.
1000686545.korean: 그리고 키는 내가 더 큰 거 같은데?

1000686546.english: If you don't know that victory depends on strength, not right or wrong, then you deserve to die.
1000686546.korean: 승패는 옳고 그름보다는 강하고 약함에 달려있는 것을 모르면 뒈져야지.

1000686547.english: Really? Maybe you're called a villain because you cling to victory more than right or wrong?
1000686547.korean: 글쎄? 옳고 그름보다 승패에 집착하니까 악당이라는 소리를 듣는 거 아닐까?

1000686550.english: Do you know how many times I've annihilated you?
1000686550.korean: 내가 몇 번이나 너희를 몰살 시켰는지 알아?

1000686552.english: The results of justice are incidental.

1000686553.english: Acting because it's right. Isn't that real justice?
1000686553.korean: 옳기에 행하는 것. 그게 진짜 정의 아닐까?

1000686554.english: What meaning does justice have for a Bioroid?

1000686555.english: What meaning does justice have for a Bioroid that must act according to its master's orders regardless of its own sense of justice!?
1000686555.korean: 주인의 말에 따라 자신의 정의에 상관없이 움직여야 하는 바이오로이드에게 정의가 무슨 의미가 있는데!?

1000686556.english: You disgusting justice-obsessed freak… the more I talk to you, the more nauseous I feel like throwing up.
1000686556.korean: 역겨운 정의병자 같으니… 너랑 이야기할수록 역겨워 토할 거 같아.

1000686558.english: Fine. I'll add a few smacks to the head as well.
1000686558.korean: 좋아. 꿀밤 몇 대도 추가해 줄게.

1000686560.english: I have talked to that girl before.
1000686560.korean: 그 년이랑 이야기한 적 있긴 하지.

1000686561.english: They said I could be happy too.

1000686562.english: I know better than anyone that I can't be happy.
1000686562.korean: 내가 행복해질 수 없는 건 내가 더 잘 아는데?

1000686563.english: Do you know who is making me unhappy before saying something like that?
1000686563.korean: 내가 누구 때문에 불행한지 알고 그런 소리를 하는건가?

1000686564.english: So do you think I'd be pissed off? Wouldn't I be?

1000686569.english: Even a single scurrying gnat is a total pain to die for!

1000686570.english: I'm going to kill them all!

1000686571.english: Kill me, kill me. Don't use such strong words.
1000686571.korean: 죽인다, 죽인다. 너무 강한 말 쓰지 말라고.

1000686572.english: Isn't it just pathetic to lose that much?

1000686575.english: I don't know what the mongooses you faced back then were like, but they're probably different now.
1000686575.korean: 옛날 네가 상대한 몽구스가 어땠는지는 몰라도 지금은 다를 걸?

1000686576.english: If you underestimate Orca's mongoose, you're going to get into trouble.
1000686576.korean: 오르카의 몽구스를 무시했다가는 험한 꼴을 당할거야.

1000686577.english: No. Are you already in trouble?
1000686577.korean: 아니다. 이미 험한 꼴을 당하고 있나?

1000686578.english: Look. You can't even catch me.
1000686578.korean: 봐. 이미 나 하나 잡지도 못 하잖아.

1000686580.english: … … I wanted to do this with the commander.

1000686581.english: Shirayuri. Please send us Pinto's and our location in real time.
1000686581.korean: 시라유리. 핀토와 저희들의 위치를 실시간으로 전송해주세요.

1000686582.english: Finn… Hero, please hold on just a little longer. I’m on my way!
1000686582.korean: 핀… 히어로 조금만 더 버텨주세요. 지금 가고 있어요!

1000686583.english: Hehe, Janghwa. I'm full of energy, so you're not already panting, are you? That would be disappointing.
1000686583.korean: 헤헤, 장화. 나는 팔팔한데 너는 벌써 헉헉거리는 거 아니지? 그러면 실망인데.

1000686585.english: You will soon reach the target location.

1000686587.english: Hero. Please guide the target to the location you just marked.
1000686587.korean: 히어로. 지금 마킹한 위치로 목표를 유도해주세요.

1000686588.english: You look tired, Janghwa? Shall we rest over there for a bit?
1000686588.korean: 힘들어 보이네, 장화? 저기에서 조금 쉬었다 갈까?

1000686589.english: This was also something I wanted to tell the commander...

1000686596.english: Do you think you've won just because you lured them into a trap like this?
1000686596.korean: 이딴 함정에 빠트렸다고 다 이긴 줄 알아?

1000686597.english: Why are you trying to fight, Janghwa?

1000686598.english: The meaning of wanting to talk to you—

1000686600.english: If you want to take us down, you'll have to take me down first!
1000686600.korean: 우리를 쓰러트리려면 나 먼저 쓰러트려야 할 걸!

1000686601.english: And if there is someone behind me, I will never fall!
1000686601.korean: 그리고 내 뒤에 누가 있다면 나는 절대로 쓰러지지 않아!

1000686602.english: Do you think you can win just because you have a lot of people!
1000686602.korean: 머릿수가 많으면 다 이길 수 있을 거라고 생각하는 거냐!

1000686608.english: Surrender? You want me to surrender?

1000686609.english: I would never surrender to a bitch like you even if I died!
1000686609.korean: 네 년한텐 죽어도 항복 안 해!

1000686610.english: - JangHwa pulled out a switch and pressed it.

1000686612.english: - JangHwa pressed the switch again, but still nothing happened.
1000686612.korean: - 장화는 다시 스위치를 눌렀지만 역시나 아무런 일도 일어나지 않았다.

1000686613.english: - Right then, a communication came in.

1000686615.english: We have defused all six bombs installed on the building's critical points.
1000686615.korean: 건물 중요 요소에 설치된 폭탄 6개소를 전부 해체했어요.

1000686616.english: We will stand by until further orders.
1000686616.korean: 별도의 명령이 있을 때까지 대기하고 있을 게요.

1000686617.english: - Having used up all the bombs and with all installed bombs defused.
1000686617.korean: - 모든 폭탄을 써버리고, 설치했던 폭탄도 전부 해체됐다.

1000686618.english: - JangHwa, having used up her last card, screamed in fury.
1000686618.korean: - 마지막 카드를 전부 써버린 장화는 악을 쓰며 외쳤다.

1000686619.english: Shoot! Kill! I'd rather die than beg you for my life!
1000686619.korean: 쏴! 죽이라고! 너한테 목숨을 구걸할 바엔 뒈지고 말지!

1000686622.english: Why do you want to die?

1000686624.english: JangHwa… I want to hear you.

1000686625.english: If I, HongRyeon, have done something wrong to you, I want to apologize.
1000686625.korean: 제가, 홍련이 당신에게 잘못한 게 있다면 사과하고 싶어요.

1000686626.english: Ha! How noble. Still so saintly to the end!

1000686628.english: Do you think apologizing will automatically make the other person understand?
1000686628.korean: 사과하면 상대가 무조건 이해할 거라고 생각해?

1000686629.english: Do you think that if someone is angry, it's because you must have done something wrong?
1000686629.korean: 상대가 화가 나면 자기가 잘못했을 거라고 생각해?

1000686630.english: Is your head full of flowers?

1000686631.english: How can I… make you stop hating me?
1000686631.korean: 어떻게 하면… 당신이 저를 싫어하는 것을 그만 둘 건가요?

1000686633.english: Sorry, but I can't do that.

1000686634.english: What is the cause of your hatred toward me?
1000686634.korean: 당신의 저에 대한 증오는 무엇이 원인이죠?

1000686635.english: What's the difference between you and me!

1000686636.english: Why are you loved and happy!

1000686637.english: No matter how many times you fail! Even if you die and a new one comes, it's the same!
1000686637.korean: 네가 아무리 실패해도! 죽고 새로운 녀석이 와도 똑같아!

1000686638.english: I can become happy! I'll help you! I want you to be happy!
1000686638.korean: 내가 행복해질 수 있다! 자신이 도와주겠다! 내가 행복하길 원한다!

1000686641.english: Because you're pure! Because you're happy! Because you can't understand my situation! That's why you can spout that kind of bullshit!
1000686641.korean: 자신은 깨끗하니까! 행복하니까! 내 처지를 이해하지 못하니까! 그딴 개소리를 내뱉을 수 있는 거잖아!

1000686642.english: You're on the side that's loved and happy! Unlike me!
1000686642.korean: 너는 사랑받고 행복한 쪽에 있으니까! 나랑 다르게!

1000686643.english: The more you are like that, the more miserable I become, and you don't even know it!
1000686643.korean: 네가 그럴수록 나는 더욱 비참해지는 것도 모르면서!

1000686644.english: ……The one who doesn't understand is you.

1000686645.english: You say I'm clean? You say I have no sin?
1000686645.korean: 제가 깨끗하다고요? 저에겐 아무런 죄가 없다고요?

1000686646.english: How much do you know about me to say that?
1000686646.korean: 당신은 저에 대해 얼마나 안다고 그런 말을 하나요?

1000686648.english: It may look that way. No. There are people who truly love me.
1000686648.korean: 그렇게 보이겠죠. 아니. 실제로 저를 사랑해주는 사람들이 있어요.

1000686649.english: But do you know that being loved brings its own pain?
1000686649.korean: 하지만 사랑 받는 만큼 고통스러운 것을 아시나요?

1000686650.english: I… am no different from you.
1000686650.korean: 저는… 저도 당신과 다를 바가 없어요.

1000686651.english: - For a moment, only the two of them's breathing could be heard beyond the screen.
1000686651.korean: - 잠시 동안 화면 너머에서는 두 사람의 숨소리만 들렸다.

1000686652.english: - After a long while, HongRyeon spoke.
1000686652.korean: - 그렇게 한참 후, 홍련이 입을 열었다.

1000686653.english: Among the units Black River established, there was one called the Ratel team.
1000686653.korean: 블랙리버가 설립한 부대 중에는 라텔 팀이라는 것이 있었어요.

1000686654.english: - When HongRyeon spoke like that, I heard Shirayuri beside me murmur softly.
1000686654.korean: - 홍련이 그렇게 입을 열자 내 옆에 있던 시라유리가 작게 중얼거리는 것이 들렸다.

1000686655.english: Are you intending to confess your sins before everyone?
1000686655.korean: 당신은 당신의 죄를 만인 앞에서 고백할 생각인가요?

1000686658.english: There are many people who, once the sins are revealed, will help you atone.
1000686658.korean: 죄를 밝힌 만큼… 당신의 속죄를 도와줄 사람도 많을 거니까요.

1000686659.english: Black River turned civilians in the anarchic regions into terrorists, and when they carried out attacks, they deployed the Ratel team.
1000686659.korean: 블랙리버는 무정부 지역의 민간인들을 테러리스트로 만들고 그들이 테러를 일으키면 라텔 팀을 투입했어요.

1000686660.english: The Ratel team was completed as they accumulated real-world data against those manufactured terrorists.
1000686660.korean: 라텔 팀은 만들어진 테러리스트들을 대상으로 실전 데이터를 쌓으며 완성되어갔죠.

1000686662.english: There were people the Ratel team had rescued.

1000686663.english: Realizing that fact, the Ratel team slowly fell apart.
1000686663.korean: 그 사실을 깨달은 라텔 팀은 서서히 망가졌어요.

1000686664.english: Seeing the people they had rescued become terrorists.
1000686664.korean: 자신들이 구조한 사람들이 테러리스트가 되는 것을 보고.

1000686665.english: Because of the contradiction of having to kill the people they had rescued and the people they should rescue.
1000686665.korean: 자신들이 구조했던 사람들, 구조해야 할 사람들을 죽여야 하는 모순 때문에.

1000686666.english: There were children who didn't know what was wrong.

1000686667.english: There were children who averted their eyes, saying it wasn't their fault.
1000686667.korean: 자기 책임이 아니라고 눈을 돌린 아이도 있었어요.

1000686668.english: There were children who came to believe this was the right thing.
1000686668.korean: 이게 옳은 일이라고 믿어버린 아이도 있었어요.

1000686669.english: There were children who obeyed orders unconditionally because they were orders.

1000686671.english: There were fools who believed that everyone must atone.
1000686671.korean: 모두가 속죄해야 한다고 믿어버린 멍청이도 있었어요.

1000686672.english: That fool reported to Black River that the Ratel team's operational efficiency was dropping — and the causes.
1000686672.korean: 그 멍청이는 라텔 팀의 작전효율이 떨어지고 있다는 것을 블랙리버에 보고 했어요. 그 원인도.

1000686673.english: What do you think Black River did?

1000686674.english: A new Ratel team trained against the inefficient Ratel team.
1000686674.korean: 새로운 라텔 팀이 효율이 떨어진 라텔 팀을 상대로 훈련을 했어요.

1000686675.english: Under the pretext of preparing for terrorists using Bioroids in attacks.
1000686675.korean: 테러리스트가 바이오로이드를 이용하는 테러를 일으켰을 때를 대비한다는 명목으로요.

1000686676.english: Because they were orders, children with the same faces killed each other.
1000686676.korean: 명령이기에 같은 얼굴을 한 아이들이 서로를 죽였어요.

1000686677.english: And… operations officers and operations officers did the same to each other.

1000686678.english: In the end, only one operations officer was left standing.

1000686679.english: I don't know which side the surviving operations officer was on.
1000686679.korean: 살아남은 작전관이 어느 쪽인지는 저도 모르겠어요.

1000686681.english: The Mongoose team was born by splitting the Ratel team in two.
1000686681.korean: 라텔 팀을 양분으로 몽구스 팀이 탄생했죠.

1000686682.english: The Ratel team Black River created was built on the blood of innocent victims.
1000686682.korean: 블랙리버가 만든 라텔 팀은 무고한 희생자들의 피로 이루어졌어요.

1000686683.english: And… I was formed from the Ratel team's blood.
1000686683.korean: 그리고… 저는 라텔 팀의 피로 이루어졌어요.

1000686684.english: No matter how I struggle, I cannot refuse human orders.
1000686684.korean: 아무리 발버둥쳐도 인간의 명령을 거부하지 못해요.

1000686685.english: No matter how I struggle, I cannot forget the sins of the past.
1000686685.korean: 아무리 발버둥쳐도 과거의 죄를 잊지 못해요.

1000686686.english: No matter how I struggle, I cannot be forgiven by the victims.
1000686686.korean: 아무리 발버둥쳐도 희생자에게 용서 받지 못해요.

1000686689.english: The original sin I gained as HongRyeon.

1000686690.english: I am searching for a way to atone.

1000686691.english: Maybe… I won't find it until I die.
1000686691.korean: 어쩌면… 제가 죽을 때까지 찾지 못할지도 모르죠.

1000686692.english: Perhaps... such a thing might not even exist.

1000686693.english: But that reason doesn't become a reason for me not to atone.
1000686693.korean: 하지만 그 이유가 제가 속죄하지 말아야 할 이유가 되지는 못해요.

1000686695.english: I killed innocent people under the Empress's orders, and I attacked the Mongoose team—can I be forgiven?
1000686695.korean: 여제의 명령에 따라 무고한 사람을 죽이고, 몽구스 팀을 공격한 내가?

1000686696.english: I can't give you a definite answer.

1000686697.english: The people you killed can no longer forgive you, nor can they refuse to forgive you.
1000686697.korean: 당신이 죽인 사람들은 더 이상 당신을 용서해주지도, 용서하지 않는 것도 못하니까요.

1000686698.english: But... if you want to atone...

1000686699.english: Let's look together. For a way to wash away our sins.
1000686699.korean: 같이 찾아봐요. 우리들의 죄를 씻을 수 있는 방법을.

1000686700.english: You didn't kill them with your own hands.

1000686701.english: I... I killed them with my own hands.

1000686702.english: So you're going to give up everything?

1000686703.english: Will you give up not committing further sins and continue piling up crimes?
1000686703.korean: 더 이상 죄 짓지 않는 것을 포기하고 계속 죄를 쌓아갈 건가요?

1000686704.english: Or will you give up everything and choose death?
1000686704.korean: 아니면 모든 것을 포기하고 죽음을 택할 건가요?

1000686705.english: Don't choose either. Don't pile up sins... don't die, JangHwa.
1000686705.korean: 어느 쪽이든 고르지 마세요. 죄를 쌓지 말고… 죽지마세요, 장화.

1000686706.english: Atonement is only possible if you live.

1000686707.english: If you die, only sin, resentment, and corpses remain.
1000686707.korean: 죽어버리면 죄와 원한, 시체만 남을 뿐이니까요.

1000686708.english: There are many people around you who will help you!
1000686708.korean: 너는 주위에 널 도와주는 사람이 많잖아!

1000686710.english: Do you think someone like me is the same as you?

1000686711.english: You said I'm loved and happy, right?

1000686713.english: No, that would be rude to the people who love me.
1000686713.korean: 아니, 이러면 저를 사랑해주는 사람들에게 실례가 되겠죠.

1000686714.english: Thanks to the people who love me, I am happy.
1000686714.korean: 저를 사랑해주는 사람들 덕분에 저는 행복해요.

1000686715.english: Then you can be too. I'm no better than you.
1000686715.korean: 그러면 당신도 가능해요. 저도 당신보다 나은 자가 아니니까.

1000686716.english: As you said, I was lucky to have good people around me.
1000686716.korean: 당신 말처럼 운 좋게 주위에 좋은 사람들이 있었으니까.

1000686718.english: JangHwa, if you think you are unhappy and unloved...
1000686718.korean: 장화, 당신이 행복하지 않고, 사랑받지 못한다고 생각하시면…

1000686720.english: I'll love you and make you happy.
1000686720.korean: 당신을 사랑해 주고, 당신을 행복하게 해줄게요.

1000686722.english: Why are you doing this for me?

1000686723.english: Not only you, why are all the HongRyeons trying to be kind to me?
1000686723.korean: 너뿐만 아니라 왜 모든 홍련이 나에게 상냥하게 해주려고 하는 건데?

1000686724.english: Just like you used to dislike me...

1000686725.english: All the HongRyeons must want to embrace you.
1000686725.korean: 홍련들 모두가 그냥 당신을 보듬어주려고 하는 거겠죠.

1000686726.english: If we look for a reason...

1000686727.english: Is it because we look alike?

1000686736.english: But… what’s the point of saying this now?
1000686736.korean: 하지만… 이제 와서 이런 이야기를 해서 무슨 의미가 있어?

1000686737.english: No matter how hard I struggled... my situation didn't improve.
1000686737.korean: 내가 아무리 발버둥쳐도... 내 처지는 나아지지 않았는데.

1000686738.english: No matter how hard I struggle... the past won't change.
1000686738.korean: 내가 아무리 발버둥쳐도... 과거는 바뀌지 않는데.

1000686739.english: Even though I am left alone in the world, I am still empty and unhappy.
1000686739.korean: 홀로 세상에 떨어져도 나는 여전히 공허하고 불행한데.

1000686740.english: If even being loved and happy requires qualifications...

1000686741.english: Do I deserve to be loved and happy?

1000686742.english: To me… who was created for revenge from the very beginning?

1000686743.english: To me… created with malice from the very beginning?

1000686744.english: Like this… to me, stained with blood?

1000686745.english: After the series of events ended, the culprit stood before me.
1000686745.korean: - 일련의 사건이 끝나고 사건의 원흉이 내 앞에 섰다.

1000686746.english: - She spoke without hiding the smile that came naturally to her now that everyone had returned safely.
1000686746.korean: - 모두가 무사히 돌아온 것에 절로 나오는 웃음을 숨기지 않고 말했다.

1000686747.english: …Is it okay to be with someone like this?

1000686748.english: She is playful but a good person.

1000686749.english: This attitude is possible because everyone is safe.
1000686749.korean: - 이런 태도도 모두가 무사하기에 가능할 것이다.

1000686750.english: If the Mongoose team or any of the members participating in the operation had died, they would not have been able to face JangHwa like this.
1000686750.korean: - 만약에 몽구스 팀 혹은 작전에 참여한 대원 중 누군가가 죽었으면 이런 식으로 장화와 마주하지 못했을 것이다.

1000686751.english: Are you sure this person isn't doing anything strange to you?
1000686751.korean: 혹시나 이 인간에게 이상한 짓을 당하고 있는 건 아니고?

1000686752.english: I'm warning you not to do anything weird.

1000686753.english: It seems like I get on the receiving end more often than I do it, but that's probably just my imagination.
1000686753.korean: - 하기보다는 당하는 빈도가 더 많은 것 같지만 기분탓 일 거다.

1000686754.english: Leaving that aside, I still felt a sense of unfamiliarity with Janghwa's attitude.
1000686754.korean: - 차치하고 장화의 태도에 나는 아직은 낯선 느낌을 받았다.

1000686755.english: - Conversing at an equal level as a person, rather than with unconditional respect.
1000686755.korean: - 무조건적인 존경이 아닌 인격체로서 동등한 눈높이로 대화를 하는 모습.

1000686756.english: What idiot would allow another human's command to work on a bioroid about to commit terrorism?
1000686756.korean: 어느 바보가 테러를 저지를 바이오로이드에게 다른 인간의 명령권이 통하도록 해?

1000686757.english: Come to think of it, that's true.

1000686758.english: - It wouldn't be impossible either. There are members like Labiata, Alpha, Dragon, and Mei who were guaranteed autonomy or the right to refuse from the start.
1000686758.korean: - 불가능하지도 않을 것이다. 라비아타, 알파, 용, 메이처럼 처음부터 자주권 혹은 거부권이 보장된 대원들도 있고.

1000686759.english: - Okay, then I have already decided what to say.
1000686759.korean: - 좋아 그러면 내가 할 말은 정해져있다.

1000686761.english: Janghwa wore a look of disbelief.

1000686762.english: The other members giggled, so I laughed and spoke as well.
1000686762.korean: - 다른 대원들은 키득키득 웃었기에 나도 웃으며 말했다.

1000686763.english: I am Janghwa. The Empress's hound. A sabotage expert belonging to the Empressis Hound.
1000686763.korean: 나는 장화. 여제님의 사냥개. 엠프레시스 하운드 소속의 사보타주 전문가.

1000686764.english: I committed terror, incited, and spread rumors on the Empress's orders.
1000686764.korean: 여제님의 명에 따라 테러를 일으키고 선동하고 유언비어를 퍼트렸어.

1000686766.english: I was about to say that I had merely followed orders, but I kept my mouth shut.
1000686766.korean: - 나는 명령에 따랐을 뿐이지 않느냐고 말을 하려다가 입을 다물었다.

1000686767.english: Hongryeon also harmed innocent people, or those who would have been innocent, on orders.
1000686767.korean: - 홍련도 명령에 따라 무고한, 무고했을 사람들을 해쳤다.

1000686768.english: However, HongRyeon was not granted absolution for doing so because of orders.
1000686768.korean: - 하지만 홍련은 명령때문에 그랬다는 면죄부를 받지 않았다.

1000686769.english: - She was reflecting on her sins and searching for a way to atone for them.
1000686769.korean: - 자신의 죄를 고찰하고, 속죄하는 방법을 찾고 있었다.

1000686770.english: - If even Hong-ryeon, who was restored by Orca and didn’t commit any crimes herself, is in that situation...
1000686770.korean: - 오르카에서 복원되어서 직접 죄를 짓지 않은 홍련도 그럴진대.

1000686771.english: - It was clear that Jang Hwa, who had committed the crime herself, would not take kindly to anyone downplaying her guilt.
1000686771.korean: - 직접 죄를 지은 장화가 자신의 죄를 가벼이 여기는 것을 탐탁지 않게 여길 것은 분명했다.

1000686772.english: Just before humanity was on the verge of extinction, the Empress ordered Angel to be killed, and following the Empress's command, I wandered the world.
1000686772.korean: 인류가 멸망하기 직전. 여제님은 앙헬을 죽이라는 명령을 내렸고, 여제님의 명에 따라 전 세계를 떠돌아다녔어.

1000686773.english: Judging by the fact that it hasn't been found so far, Angel must be dead, too.
1000686773.korean: 지금까지 발견 못한 것을 보면 앙헬도 죽었겠지.

1000686775.english: What are you going to do going forward?

1000686777.english: There was no way for meaning to suddenly appear to me, who had been wandering the world meaninglessly.
1000686777.korean: 무의미하게 세상을 떠돌아다니던 나에게 갑자기 의미가 생길 리 없-

1000686779.english: - Jang-hwa, who had been speaking as if to herself, fell silent.
1000686779.korean: - 반쯤 혼잣말을 하던 장화는 그대로 입을 다물었다.

1000686780.english: - However, having overheard the conversation between Jang-hwa and Hong-ryeon, I could guess what she was thinking.
1000686780.korean: - 그러나 장화와 홍련의 대화를 들은 나는 그녀가 무슨 생각을 떠올렸는지 짐작이 갔다.

1000686782.english: That is why I didn't pry any further.
1000686782.korean: - 그러했기에 나는 이 이상 캐묻지 않았다.

1000686783.english: So you're saying I should wash up, eat, and get plenty of rest.

1000686785.english: Let's ask about various things later when we have a little more time.
1000686785.korean: - 나중에 좀 더 여유가 생기면 이것저것 더 물어보자.

1000686786.english: Let's go, Janghwa. After you rest first, I will give you a tour of the Orca.
1000686786.korean: 가요, 장화. 먼저 쉰 후에 제가 오르카 호를 안내해 드릴게요.

1000686788.english: HongRyeon took Jang-hwa and left my room.
1000686788.korean: - 홍련은 장화를 데리고 내 방을 나섰다.

1000686789.english: There was no trace in their appearance of having been pointing weapons at each other until recently.
1000686789.korean: - 둘의 모습에선 얼마 전까지 무기를 겨누던 사이라는 흔적을 찾아볼 수 없었다.

1000686790.english: After letting out a sigh of relief that all the incidents had ended safely.
1000686790.korean: - 무사히 모든 사건이 끝난 것에 안도의 한숨을 내쉰 후.

1000686792.english: Everyone is waiting in the conference room right now.
1000686792.korean: 지금 회의실에서 모두가 기다리고 있는 중이야.

1000686793.english: When I arrived at the conference room, everyone was waiting for me.
1000686793.korean: - 회의실에 도착하니 모두가 나를 기다리고 있었다.

1000686794.english: After sitting down, I nodded toward the doctor.
1000686794.korean: - 나는 자리에 앉은 후에 닥터를 향해 고개를 끄덕였다.

1000686795.english: The doctor manipulated the panel, and a message appeared on the screen.
1000686795.korean: - 닥터가 패널을 조작하더니 화면에 통신문이 떠올랐다.

1000686797.english: ask for your forgiveness in advance for not being able to reveal my identity.
1000686797.korean: 제 정체를 밝히지 못하는 점에 대해서는 미리 용서를 구합니다.

1000686798.english: If Lemonade Omega were to decipher this communication, not only I but also a considerable number of bioroids would be killed, so I had no choice but to hide it.
1000686798.korean: 만약에 레모네이드 오메가가 이 통신을 해독하면 저뿐만 아니라 적잖은 바이오로이드들이 죽임을 당하기에 부득의하게 숨길 수밖에 없었습니다.

1000686799.english: To get straight to the point, there are many bioroids who want to join Orca after seeing the propaganda of the Orca Resistance.
1000686799.korean: 곧장 본론으로 들어가자면 오르카 저항군의 선전을 보고 오르카에 합류하고자 하는 바이오로이드들이 많습니다.

1000686800.english: However, even if they want to join Orca, it is difficult to secure the means to get there, and bioroids under the control of PECS are being summarily executed just for possessing Orca's propaganda videos.
1000686800.korean: 하지만 오르카에 합류하고자 하더라도 오르카까지 갈 수단을 확보하기 힘든 지경이고, PECS의 지배권에 있는 바이오로이드들은 오르카의 선전 영상을 소지만 해도 즉결 처형을 당하고 있습니다.

1000686804.english: To gain trust, I am attaching and sending some internal information from PECS.
1000686804.korean: 신뢰를 얻기 위해 PECS 내부의 몇몇 정보를 첨부해서 보냅니다.

1000686805.english: Please send your response to the following location.

1000686806.english: After reading the entire message, I looked at Alpha.
1000686806.korean: - 통신문을 전부 읽은 후, 나는 알파를 바라보았다.

1000686808.english: That is indeed PECS's secret information. I didn't even know the location of Omega's secret lab.
1000686808.korean: PECS의 비밀 정보가 맞아요. 심지어 오메가의 비밀 연구소 위치 같은 건 저도 몰랐던 거네요.

1000686809.english: Looking at this, the sender seems to be someone holding a fairly high position within PECS.
1000686809.korean: 이걸 보면 발신자는 PECS 내부에서 꽤나 고위직에 있는 자 같아요.

1000686810.english: Is there no possibility that it's a trap?

1000686811.english: It wouldn't be strange if something happened.
1000686811.korean: 무슨 일이 일어나도 이상할 건 없어요.

1000686812.english: However, the probability that an Omega who has lost Kestos Himas will take immediate action is not high.
1000686812.korean: 하지만 케스토스 히마스를 잃은 오메가가 곧장 행동으로 옮길 확률은 높지 않아요.

1000686813.english: It is certain that our propaganda campaign is having an effect.
1000686813.korean: 우리들의 선전전이 효과를 발휘하고 있는 것은 확실하죠.

1000686814.english: In this situation, Omega would prioritize internal control and focus on using physical force after building up its military strength.
1000686814.korean: 이런 상황이라면 오메가는 내부 단속을 우선시하고, 군사력을 키운 후에 물리력을 행사하는 쪽에 집중하겠죠.

1000686815.english: You mentioned a high-ranking official at PECS; do you have any idea who it is?
1000686815.korean: PECS의 고위직이라고 했는데, 짐작가는 이가 있소?

1000686816.english: It’s not many, but it’s not few either. After all, there are many bioroids who have no choice but to obey Omegas.
1000686816.korean: 많지는 않지만 적지도 않아요. 어쩔 수 없이 오메가에게 복종하는 바이오로이드도 많으니까요.

1000686817.english: - Come to think of it, it would be stranger if there weren't any subordinates who held a grudge against her, given her personality.
1000686817.korean: - 하긴 그 성격에 원한을 가진 부하가 없는 게 더 이상하지.

1000686818.english: Fortunately, I have a reliable source in North America, so I will cross-verify the information through them.
1000686818.korean: 마침 북미에는 믿을 만한 정보원이 있으니 정보원을 통해 정보의 교차 검증을 하겠어요.

1000686819.english: If it's not a trap, what would you do, Master?

1000686820.english: At Labiata's words, everyone's eyes turned to me.
1000686820.korean: - 라비아타의 말에 모두의 시선이 나에게 모였다.

1000686821.english: There is no need to think too long about it.

1000686822.english: When we consider the reason for our propaganda campaign, we absolutely cannot ignore those asking for salvation.
1000686822.korean: - 우리가 선전전을 한 이유를 생각하면 절대로 구원을 요청하는 이들을 무시할 수 없다.

1000686823.english: Because we must not stop at merely giving hope, but actually take action so that others will be able to trust us.
1000686823.korean: - 희망만 주는 것으로 끝나는 것이 아니라 실제로 행동으로 옮겨야 다른 이들도 우리를 믿을 수 있을 것이니까.

1000686825.english: Someone asks for help. Because it is the right thing to do to help them.
1000686825.korean: - 누군가가 도움을 요청한다. 그러면 도움을 주는 것이 옳은 일이니까.

1000686826.english: - This is one of the values I learned at Orca.
1000686826.korean: - 내가 오르카에서 배운 가치 중 하나다.

1000686827.english: Then, in accordance with my master's orders, a rescue requester... no, it would be more accurate to call them a refugee.
1000686827.korean: 그러면 주인님의 명에 따라 구조요청자… 아니, 난민이라고 칭하는 것이 더 옳겠네요.

1000686828.english: will plan an operation to rescue refugees.

1000686829.english: To do so, it would be right to establish a base.
1000686829.korean: 그러기 위해선 거점을 마련하는 것이 옳을 것이오.

1000686830.english: And Yokohama, where we are located, is a region that is easy to establish as a base.
1000686830.korean: 그리고 마침 우리가 있는 요코하마는 거점화하기 용이한 지역이오.

1000686831.english: First of all, I will annihilate the remaining Iron Bug in Yokohama.
1000686831.korean: 우선적으로 요코하마에 있는 철충 잔당들을 섬멸하겠소.

1000686832.english: - Ignorance became courage that does not know retreat.

1000686834.english: Hehe, don't worry. My shield is sturdy, and I'm sturdier!
1000686834.korean: 헤헤, 걱정마. 내 방패는 튼튼하고, 나는 더 튼튼하니까!

1000686835.english: - Indifference became prudence that waits for an opportunity.

1000686836.english: Just a little. Just a little.

1000686838.english: Tango down! Took out the big one!

1000686840.english: Found Metal Parasite artillery unit at the rearmost position!

1000686841.english: I'll transmit coordinates to the fleet!

1000686842.english: - Obedience became the responsibility of doing one's duty.
1000686842.korean: - 복종은 본분을 다 하는 책임이 되었다.

1000686843.english: Wait, Pinto! You went too far forward!

1000686844.english: Fall back behind Draco, even for reorganization!

1000686845.english: - If the Ratel team's operations officer hadn't committed the arrogance of taking the sin upon herself alone…
1000686845.korean: - 라텔 팀의 작전관이 홀로 죄를 지고 가겠다는 오만을 저지르지 않았다면…

1000686846.english: - The Ratel team's members would have been different too.

1000686847.english: - A wrong judgement stole their chance to change and their chance to atone.
1000686847.korean: - 그릇된 판단으로 그녀들이 변할 기회도, 속죄할 기회도 빼앗아버렸다.

1000686848.english: - Wrong choices are the operations officer's responsibility.

1000686849.english: - Even if not the person directly involved…

1000686850.english: - If you inherit the Ratel team operations officer's legacy, you must inherit her responsibilities and original sin as well.
1000686850.korean: - 라텔 팀 작전관의 유산을 물려받았다면 그녀의 책임과 원죄도 물려받아야 할 것이다.

1000686853.english: Huh? Were you hit by shrapnel?

1000686856.english: About what you talked with JangHwa…

1000686857.english: I won't say I don't care. That would be a lie.
1000686857.korean: 신경 쓰지 않는다는 말은 하지 않을게요. 거짓말이 될 테니까.

1000686858.english: But that fact doesn't erase my respect and affection for you.
1000686858.korean: 하지만 그 사실 때문에 엄마에 대한 존경과 애정이 사라지지는 않아요.

1000686859.english: If you had truly been a villain…

1000686860.english: You wouldn't have worried about your own sins.
1000686860.korean: 자신의 죄에 대해서 고민하지 않으셨을 테니까요.

1000686861.english: As you said, there's no one who can forgive your sins.
1000686861.korean: 엄마가 했던 말씀처럼 엄마의 죄를 용서해줄 수 있는 사람은 없어요.

1000686862.english: But there are people beside you who will help your atonement.
1000686862.korean: 하지만 엄마의 곁에는 엄마의 속죄를 도와줄 사람이 있잖아요.

1000686863.english: We are here, and Orca's friends are here.

1000686864.english: Don't shoulder your sins alone like a penitent.

1000686865.english: The more you suffer alone, the sadder we become.
1000686865.korean: 엄마가 혼자 고통스러워 하시는 만큼 저희들도 슬퍼져요.

1000686866.english: I won't say "What does it matter what the old me did wrong?"
1000686866.korean: 옛날의 다른 내가 잘못한 게 무슨 상관이야? 같은 소리는 안 할 거예요.

1000686867.english: If there is no learning from failures and mistakes, those failures and mistakes become meaningless.
1000686867.korean: 실패와 실수에서 배움이 없다면 실패와 실수는 아무런 의미가 없는 것이 되어버리니까요.

1000686873.english: - There is no one left who can forgive my sins.
1000686873.korean: - 나의 죄를 용서해줄 이는 더 이상 없으니까.

1000686874.english: - I do not know how to atone for my sins.
1000686874.korean: - 나는 나의 죄를 속죄하는 방법을 모른다.

1000686875.english: - I don't know if I can find it before I die.
1000686875.korean: - 내가 죽기 전까지 찾을 수 있을지도 알 수 없다.

1000686877.english: - But I will not give up searching for a way to atone.
1000686877.korean: - 그러나 나는 속죄하는 방법을 찾는 것을 포기하지 않을 것이다.

1000686878.english: - It is the least memorial for those sacrificed so that the Mongoose team and I could exist.
1000686878.korean: - 그것이 몽구스 팀과 내가 있을 수 있게 희생된 자들에 대한 최소한의 위령이니까.

1000686880.english: - It is the smallest gratitude and repayment to those precious ones who love and cheer for me.
1000686880.korean: - 나를 사랑해주고 응원해주는 소중한 이들에 대한 최소한의 감사와 보은이니까.

1000686881.english: - JangHwa provided us with information about the Empress Hound after resting for two days.
1000686881.korean: - 장화는 이틀을 쉰 후에 우리에게 엠프레시스 하운드에 대한 정보를 제공했다.

1000686882.english: - That's all I know about the Empress Hound.
1000686882.korean: -까지가 내가 엠프레시스 하운드에 대해서 알고 있는 전부야.

1000686883.english: Some of the ones I mentioned are probably already dead. Ah, and the last one I mentioned is definitely dead. I found her corpse.
1000686883.korean: 아마 내가 말한 녀석들 몇은 이미 죽었을 거야. 아, 그리고 마지막에 말한 애는 확실히 죽었어. 걔 시체를 내가 발견했으니까.

1000686884.english: - Saying that, JangHwa rolled her eyes as if surprised.
1000686884.korean: - 그렇게 말하고 장화는 신기하다는 듯이 눈을 굴렸다.

1000686885.english: Not as bad as I thought?

1000686886.english: Damn— I thought the Empress would have planted an order not to leak information about the Empress Hound.
1000686886.korean: 마귀할- 여제님이 엠프레시스 하운드에 대한 정보를 유출하지 말라는 명령을 심어 놨을 거라고 생각했거든.

1000686887.english: The more constraints you impose like that, the narrower the range of action becomes.
1000686887.korean: 그런 식으로 제약을 많이 걸면 걸수록 행동폭이 좁아지니까요.

1000686888.english: And if such constraints are imposed, it's convenient when interrogating too. If someone can't talk about a certain topic, it's easy to reveal their identity, and there are many ways to obtain information by detour.
1000686888.korean: 그리고 그런 제약이 걸려 있으면 심문할 때도 편하거든요. 특정 주제에 대해서 말하지 못하면 정체를 밝히기 쉽고, 우회해서 정보를 얻어낼 방법도 많으니까요.

1000686895.english: Ah, Charlotte is making buttered potatoes at the wharf, so go ask her for some.
1000686895.korean: 아, 샬럿이 부두에서 버터 감자 만들고 있으니까 가서 달라고 해.

1000686896.english: Charlotte's buttered potatoes are really delicious. You must eat them?
1000686896.korean: 샬럿이 만든 버터 감자 엄청 맛있으니까. 꼭 먹어야 해?

1000686900.english: When you're in a bad mood, smile! Smile!
1000686900.korean: 그냥 기분 나쁠 때는 스마일! 스마일!

1000686901.english: No matter how bad you feel, if you smile you'll feel better.
1000686901.korean: 아무리 기분 나쁜 일이 있어도 웃으면 기분이 나아져.

1000686905.english: JangHwa, want buttered potatoes? Charlotte is handing them out over there.
1000686905.korean: 장화, 버터 감자 먹을래? 저기서 샬럿이 나눠주고 있는데.

1000686907.english: Ah. Are you still bothered by what was said during the last battle?
1000686907.korean: 아. 지난 번 전투 때 했던 말 신경 쓰고 있는 거야?

1000686908.english: Eh, I was half serious. You have to separate work and private life!
1000686908.korean: 에이, 반만 진심이었어. 공과 사는 구분해야지!

1000686909.english: ……Still, JangHwa, you've had a lot of worries too, right?

1000686910.english: If you have any worries, tell me. I'll listen as much as you need.
1000686910.korean: 무슨 고민이 있으면 말해줘. 얼마든지 들어줄게.

1000686911.english: Not just me, everyone in Orca will listen.
1000686911.korean: 나뿐만 아니라 오르카의 모두가 들어줄 거야.

1000686912.english: Ah. Except Spriggan. If it gets into her ears it spreads across all of Orca.
1000686912.korean: 아. 스프리건은 빼고. 걔 귀에 들어가면 오르카 전체에 퍼진다고 봐야 해.

1000686914.english: JangHwa. Mom was looking for you, did you meet?

1000686916.english: You can meet her if you go to that street.
1000686916.korean: 저기 거리에 가면 만날 수 있을 거야.

1000686917.english: If you don't know, I'll go with you.

1000686920.english: Why are you taking care of me like this?

1000686922.english: If someone needs help, of course you help them.

1000686925.english: JangHwa. I've been looking for you.

1000686927.english: The Commander told me to give you this.

1000686929.english: It's an ID token. It's temporary, but there will be no difference when an official one is made later.
1000686929.korean: 인식증이에요. 임시이기는 하지만 나중에 정식으로 만들었을 때랑 차이가 없을 거예요.

1000686934.english: But that's not Orca's problem, it's my problem.
1000686934.korean: 하지만 그건 오르카의 문제가 아니라 내 문제야.

1000686935.english: What's the problem? Tell us and we'll help as much as possible.

1000686936.english: You know. You know what's wrong.

1000686937.english: There are sometimes strange kids, but… Orca only has good kids.
1000686937.korean: 가끔 이상한 애들도 있지만… 오르카에는 좋은 애들밖에 없네.

1000686939.english: They fought with me not long ago, but now they approach me first and take care of this and that.
1000686939.korean: 얼마 전에 나랑 싸웠으면서 지금은 나에게 먼저 다가와서 이것저것 챙겨줘.

1000686940.english: When I asked why they do that… they said it's natural.
1000686940.korean: 내가 왜 그러냐고 물어보니까… 그게 당연한 거래.

1000686941.english: What isn't natural to me is natural to them.
1000686941.korean: 나한테는 당연하지 않은 게 그 애들에게는 당연해.

1000686942.english: Before, no, until recently I was angry about that fact… but now…
1000686942.korean: 옛날에는, 아니, 얼마 전까지는 그 사실에 화가 났는데… 지금은…

1000686944.english: You asked me if life in Orca is uncomfortable?

1000686945.english: No. I'm satisfied. I could say I'm the happiest I've ever been in my life so far.
1000686945.korean: 아냐. 만족스러워. 지금까지의 삶에서 가장 행복했다고 말할 수 있을 정도로.

1000686947.english: The happier I am... the more it hurts. Like the two sides of a coin.
1000686947.korean: 행복한 만큼... 고통스러워. 마치 동전의 앞면과 뒷면처럼.

1000686948.english: When I'm here, faces identical to the kids I killed come up to me and ask if I'm okay, if I've eaten, let's hang out… They say those things.
1000686948.korean: 여기에 있으면 내가 죽인 애들이랑 같은 얼굴을 한 애들이 나에게 다가와서 잘 지내냐고, 밥은 먹었냐고, 같이 놀자고… 그런 말을 해.

1000686949.english: There's no malice or mockery, but meeting them hurts.
1000686949.korean: 악의나 비꼼은 없지만 그 애들과 마주치면 고통스러워.

1000686950.english: And now, the face I hated most is worrying about me.
1000686950.korean: 그리고 지금 내 앞에 내가 가장 증오했던 얼굴이 나를 걱정해주고 있어.

1000686951.english: In this situation, can I be fully happy?
1000686951.korean: 이런 상황에서 내가 온전히 행복할 수 있을까?

1000686952.english: You said before there's no one who can forgive our sins, right?
1000686952.korean: 전에 넌 우리의 죄를 용서해줄 이는 없다고 했잖아?

1000686953.english: But there are people who cannot forgive our sins who still remain.
1000686953.korean: 하지만 우리의 죄를 용서하지 못하는 자들은 남아있어.

1000686954.english: You know who that is, don't you?

1000686956.english: Guilt. The blood on my hands. The vengeful spirits clinging to my soul ask whether I deserve to be happy.
1000686956.korean: 죄책감이. 내 손에 묻은 피가. 내 영혼에 달라붙은 원혼이 나에게 행복해질 자격이 있냐고 물어봐.

1000686957.english: As far as I can see… I don't think I deserve it yet.
1000686957.korean: 내가 보기에는… 아직은 자격이 없는 것 같아.

1000686958.english: Do I really… have to leave?

1000686959.english: I haven't done anything for you yet…

1000686961.english: If it weren't for HongRyeon, I'd still be wandering the world nursing unreasonable grudges, wouldn't I?
1000686961.korean: 홍련이 없었으면 나는 아직도 불합리한 원한을 품고 세상을 돌아다녔을 텐데?

1000686964.english: I'm not leaving because I don't want to be happy. I'm leaving because I want to become happy.
1000686964.korean: 나는 행복해지기 싫어서 떠나는 게 아니야. 행복해지고 싶어서 떠나는 거야.

1000686965.english: You said that not knowing how to atone isn't a reason to give up atonement.
1000686965.korean: 언니가 속죄하는 방법을 모른다고 속죄하는 것을 포기해선 안 된다고 했잖아.

1000686966.english: Likewise, I think not knowing how to be happy isn't a reason to give up on becoming happy.
1000686966.korean: 그것처럼 나도 행복해지는 방법을 모른다고 행복해지는 것을 포기해서는 안 된다고 생각해.

1000686967.english: I might not find the answer until I die. Maybe there is no answer. But I won't give up.
1000686967.korean: 답은 내가 죽을 때까지 못 찾을 수도 있어. 어쩌면 답이 없을 수도 있고. 하지만 나는 포기 안 할 거야.

1000686968.english: And if I find a way to be happy… I'll come back to you.
1000686968.korean: 그리고 행복해질 방법을 찾으면… 언니 곁으로 돌아올게.

1000686972.english: Stop it, I'm not a child.

1000686976.english: - Hong-ryeon watched her back for a moment, then turned to look at me.
1000686976.korean: - 홍련은 그 뒷모습을 바라보다가 나를 바라보았다.

1000686977.english: Yes. Thank you for preparing the ID token for JangHwa.
1000686977.korean: 네. 장화를 위해서 인식증을 준비해 주셔서 감사합니다.

1000686978.english: Still, I'm glad she didn't leave without saying anything.
1000686978.korean: 그래도 아무 말 없이 떠나지 않아서 다행이에요.

1000686979.english: I tried to grab her but…

1000686980.english: JangHwa is leaving of her own will, to search for the answer to her questions—how could I, who is also trying to find answers, stop that?
1000686980.korean: 장화가 자신의 의지로, 자신의 의문에 대한 답을 찾으러 떠나는 걸, 역시나 답을 찾으려고 하는 제가 어떻게 막겠어요?

1000686981.english: As an older sister should… I have to see her off.

1000686982.english: Yes… it's a pity. I wanted to talk more and take care of her more.
1000686982.korean: 네… 아쉬워요. 좀 더 대화를 나누고 싶고 좀 더 이것저것 챙겨주고 싶었어요.

1000686984.english: That JangHwa will find a way to be happy.

1000686985.english: That JangHwa will return to our side.

1000686986.english: And that she will be happy with us…

1000686988.english: < Rose Blooming on the Lotus > END.

1000686990.english: When you come back, let's go on a date.

1000686991.english: Ah, no. You worked hard. So…

1000686992.english: I want everyone to return with smiles.

1000686994.english: Kekekeke, I am the supreme commander of Orca.

1000686995.english: Not something to say in front of the person involved…

1000686996.english: Does human command authority not work?

1000686997.english: I am the last and the first human. Orca's supreme commander.
1000686997.korean: 나는 마지막이자 첫번째 인간. 오르카의 총사령관.

1000686998.english: Someone's brother, guest, lord, friend, master…
1000686998.korean: 누군가의 오빠이자 손님, 주공, 친구, 주인님…

1000686999.english: Introductions. JangHwa, I want to know about you.
1000686999.korean: 통성명. 장화, 너에 대해서 알고 싶어서.

1000687001.english: Kekeke. From now on I'll dunk you in hot water.

1000687002.english: And slowly think about what you want to do going forward.
1000687002.korean: 그리고 앞으로 어쩌고 싶은 지 천천히 생각해봐.

1000687003.english: Doctor. Is the comms decryption finished?

1000687005.english: Of course we have to go rescue them.

1000687007.english: Did you give her the temporary ID token?

1000687008.english: JangHwa said she would leave first.

1000687009.english: You're not going to stop her?

1000687011.english: And have Sowan feed her food she can't finish by herself.
1000687011.korean: 그리고 소완을 시켜 혼자서 전부 먹지 못할 음식을 먹여주마.

1000687012.english: Finally tuck her into a fluffy blanket she can't get out of.
1000687012.korean: 마지막으로 헤어나오지 못하는 푹신한 이불에 넣어주지.

1000687019.english: Child labor. It's a heavy word, isn't it.

1000687020.english: Of course, children helping with adult chores or running errands at home is something that has existed since the beginning of humanity.
1000687020.korean: 물론 어린아이가 어른의 일손을 돕고, 가정 내에서 심부름을 하는 것은 인류의 시작과 함께 한 일일 것입니다.

1000687021.english: But the child labor we're discussing this time is not that level of work. We're talking about the murderous intensity of labor that began with the Industrial Revolution.
1000687021.korean: 하지만 저희가 이번에 이야기할 아동 노동은 그런 수준의 일이 아닙니다. 산업 혁명때부터 시작된, 살인적인 강도의 노동을 이야기하려는 겁니다.

1000687022.english: At that time, children's average daily working hours exceeded 12 hours, and in severe cases exceeded 16 hours.
1000687022.korean: 당시 아동들의 일일 평균 노동 시간은 12시간을 넘었으며, 심하면 16시간을 넘어가는 일도 있었습니다.

1000687023.english: Working seven days a week, they were paid only 5 shillings. That was about one-third of a normal worker's wage.
1000687023.korean: 그렇게 주 7일을 일하고 받는 돈은 단 5실링. 일반적인 노동자 임금의 3분의 1 수준이었죠.

1000687024.english: Hundreds of years have passed since then, but sadly the situation hasn't improved much.
1000687024.korean: 그로부터 수백 년이 지났지만, 안타깝게도 상황은 그리 나아지지 않았습니다.

1000687025.english: In mines, on farms, and even on battlefields... children are still being overworked.
1000687025.korean: 광산에서, 농장에서, 그리고 더 나아가 전쟁터에서... 아이들은 아직 혹사당하고 있습니다.

1000687026.english: Where cheap labor is needed, children's lives are being thrown away as worthless.
1000687026.korean: 값싼 노동력이 필요한 곳에서 아이들의 목숨이 무가치하게 버려지고 있는 것이죠.

1000687027.english: Now it's time to put an end to that tragedy.
1000687027.korean: 이제 그 비극에 종지부를 찍을 때입니다.

1000687028.english: No more children will suffer from harsh labor. Because now we have this small but reliable friend.
1000687028.korean: 더 이상 아이들이 가혹한 노동에 시달릴 일은 없을 겁니다. 이제 우리에겐 이 작지만 든든한 친구가 있기 때문이죠.

1000687032.english: The Dutch Girl has astonishing strength that seems impossible from such a tiny body. She can lift heavy stones with ease.
1000687032.korean: 더치걸은 그 작디 작은 몸에서 낼 수 있을 거라고는 믿을 수 없을 정도로 강력한 힘을 가지고 있습니다. 무거운 돌덩이도 번쩍 들어올릴 수 있을 정도죠.

1000687033.english: This Bioroid, with physical abilities comparable to the greatest athletes, can perfectly handle the brutal labor that children used to do.
1000687033.korean: 감히 최고의 운동선수들과 비견될 수 있는 육체능력을 가진 이 바이오로이드는, 기존 아동들이 하던 가혹한 노동을 완벽히 소화할 수 있습니다.

1000687034.english: I dare say, everyone... at this moment, the human rights of children worldwide have advanced one step!
1000687034.korean: 감히 말하건대, 여러분... 지금 이 순간, 전 세계 아동들의 인권이 한 단계 진보한 겁니다!

1000687039.english: What is the price of that Bioroid? From what I've heard, it sounds quite expensive.
1000687039.korean: 그 바이오로이드의 가격은 어떻게 되죠? 얼핏 들었을 때는 꽤 값이 나갈 것 같은데요.

1000687040.english: Ah, the price. An important factor indeed. But don't worry. Dutch Girl is a very... economical model.
1000687040.korean: 아, 가격. 중요한 요소죠. 걱정하지 마십시오. 더치걸은 매우... 경제적인 모델이거든요.

1000687041.english: If a Tommy Worker breaks down, what do you do? You contact the manufacturer, get it repaired, and pay the repair fee, right?
1000687041.korean: 토미 워커가 고장나면 어떻게 해야 할까요? 업체에 연락을 해서 수리를 받고, 수리비를 지불해야겠죠.

1000687042.english: But Dutch Girl doesn't need that. If she breaks down, just dispose of her and buy a new one. That's how affordable she is.
1000687042.korean: 하지만 더치걸은 그럴 필요가 없습니다. 더치걸이 고장나면, 그냥 폐기하고 새로운 더치걸을 구매하면 됩니다. 그게 가능할 정도로 가격이 저렴하거든요.

1000687043.english: Considering that the main reason child labor exists is because of children's cheap labor, Dutch Girl can truly replace all the tasks children used to do. So, what do you think?
1000687043.korean: 아동 노동자가 생기는 주된 이유가 아이들의 값싼 노동력 때문이라는 사실을 고려했을 때, 더치걸은 정말로 아이들이 할 수 있는 일을 전부 대체할 수 있습니다. 자, 어떠신가요?

1000687044.english: I like it very much. What about the disposal process...

1000687045.english: Of course, PECS provides disposal services for our customers as well.
1000687045.korean: 물론 PECS에서 고객들을 위해 폐기 절차도 서비스 해 드립니다.

1000687048.english: No more questions? None? What a relief. I'm a scholar by nature, so I'm not good at unscripted Q&A like this...
1000687048.korean: 더 질문 없으십니까? 없으시다고요? 와, 천만 다행이군요. 제가 천성이 학자인지라, 이런 대본 없는 질의응답에 약해서...

1000687050.english: Then I'll be leaving now. Thank you for attending...
1000687050.korean: 그럼, 이만 퇴장하도록 하겠습니다. 참석해주셔서 감사합니다...

1000687051.english: Members of the Child Rights Committee.

1000687052.english: -- In conclusion... Dutch Girl sold like wildfire.

1000687053.english: - For mining developers, the Dutch Girl was a revolution. She was cheap, durable, and an ethical product approved by the Human Rights Commission; above all, it could extract sufficient quantities of ore without the need for expensive heavy machinery.
1000687053.korean: - 광산 개발업자들에게 있어 더치걸은 혁명이었다. 싸고, 튼튼한데다, 인권위원회의 승인을 받은 윤리적인 제품이고, 무엇보다 비싼 중장비 없이도 충분한 양의 광석을 채굴했으니까.

1000687054.english: -- Some miners who lost their jobs staged protests, but they were quickly suppressed. In a situation where cheap labor was supplied infinitely, a strike no longer had any meaning.
1000687054.korean: - 그 과정에서 일자리를 잃은 광부들이 시위를 벌이기도 했지만, 금방 진압되었다. 값싼 노동력이 무제한으로 공급되는 상황에서, 파업 선언은 더 이상 아무런 의미가 없었으니까.

1000687057.english: ...That's complicated, I don't really get it.

1000687058.english: I only learned because I read the book in the break room.
1000687058.korean: 나도 휴게실에 있는 책 보고 안 거야.

1000687059.english: You should ease up on work too. When you get the chance, read a book or two.
1000687059.korean: 너도 일은 적당히 하고, 짬 나면 책도 좀 읽고 그래.

1000687061.english: Even if we work hard, it's not like we get anything extra, right? We already work 14 hours a day, and a few more hours won't get us more food...
1000687061.korean: 어차피 열심히 일해봐야 우리한테 떨어지는 것도 없잖아? 안 그래도 하루에 14시간씩 일하는데, 여기서 몇 시간 더 일한다고 해서 밥을 더 주는 것도 아니니까...

1000687062.english: So take it easy, take it easy. Even if they don't like it, the worst that happens is we get sent to a construction site.
1000687062.korean: 쉬엄쉬엄 하라고, 쉬엄쉬엄. 그러다가 밉보인다고 해도 공사판이나 가고 말겠지.

1000687064.english: Then maybe I'll rest a bit too...

1000687065.english: Rest? Don't even think about it!

1000687067.english: Supervisor? You haven't come down in ages—what's the occasion...
1000687067.korean: 감독관? 여태 안 내려오다가 무슨 일로...

1000687068.english: If I don't supervise, you slack off. Just look now... reading a book during work hours?
1000687068.korean: 감독 안 하면 너희가 놀잖아. 지금도 봐... 근무 시간에 무슨 책을 읽고 있어?

1000687070.english: Of course. We mined the amount marked here.
1000687070.korean: 그럼, 여기 표시된 양만큼 다 채굴했어.

1000687072.english: Fine. Get going. But if I catch you again, you'll regret it.
1000687072.korean: 좋아. 가 봐. 하지만 다음에 걸리면 혼날 줄 알아.

1000687073.english: Sigh... this is why old Dutch Girl models are such a headache...
1000687073.korean: 에휴, 이래서 오래 된 더치걸 모델들은 골치 아프다니까요...

1000687074.english: Exactly. There are some... side effects we didn't anticipate.
1000687074.korean: 그러게요. 저희 측에서 예상하지 못한... 부작용이 좀 있네요.

1000687075.english: It's good they're smart enough to understand orders, but they keep trying to be clever.
1000687075.korean: 똑똑해서 명령을 잘 알아듣는 건 좋습니다. 그런데 자꾸 잔꾀를 부린다니까요?

1000687076.english: They slip in low-quality ore, or stash some previously mined minerals and then pull them out during inspections like today...
1000687076.korean: 품질 떨어지는 광석을 슬쩍 섞어 넣기도 하고... 전에 채굴한 광물 중 일부를 숨겨 놨다가 지금처럼 할당량 검사를 할 때 미리 내놓기도 하고...

1000687077.english: Newcomers like No. 582 are fine, but after about five years, they all turn like this. Isn't there any solution?
1000687077.korean: 저기 저 582번처럼 새로 들어온 녀석들은 좀 괜찮은 편인데, 한 5년쯤 지난 모델들은 다 저래요. 어떻게 방법이 없겠습니까?

1000687078.english: First of all, PECS offers our sincerest apologies. But in this case, the issue arose because we mixed in so much diverse genetic data to enhance performance...
1000687078.korean: 일단, 저희 PECS 사에서 심심한 사과의 말씀을 드립니다. 하지만, 이 경우 능력 향상을 위해 유전정보를 워낙 다양하게 섞다 보니 생긴 일로...

1000687079.english: We'd recommend simply disposing of them and purchasing new ones.
1000687079.korean: 폐기하고 새 제품을 구매하시는 방법을 추천해 드리고 싶습니다.

1000687080.english: Well, they're cheap enough for that... and you get your investment back quickly anyway. So I guess it's not a big issue...
1000687080.korean: 하긴, 그래도 될 정도로 싸죠... 좀 굴리다 보면 투자한 돈도 다 회수되는 판이고. 그래서 사실 이게 큰 문제까지는 아닌데...

1000687081.english: Still, it feels wasteful to throw away ones that are still running strong.
1000687081.korean: 그래도 아직 쌩쌩한 것들을 폐기하긴 좀 아깝단 말이죠.

1000687083.english: Well... forget disposal. Let's just place an additional order for two more.
1000687083.korean: 뭐... 폐기는 됐고, 두 개 정도만 추가 발주 넣어 보죠.

1000687084.english: Sigh, I heard the Dutch Girls working at those Nordic mines have been going strong for over seven years...
1000687084.korean: 에휴, 저기 저 북유럽 쪽 광산에서 일하는 더치걸들은 7년이 넘도록 빠릿빠릿하게 일을 잘 한다던데...

1000687086.english: Yes, that's what I've been told.

1000687087.english: Interesting. Could you tell me more about that?
1000687087.korean: 흥미롭네요. 혹시 그 얘기 좀 더 해 주실 수 있나요?

1000687088.english: Mm, honestly I only heard it in passing, so I don't know the details. Ah, right. I'll give you their contact info, so you can ask directly.
1000687088.korean: 으음, 사실 저도 그냥 지나가듯 들은 얘기라 잘은 모릅니다. 아, 그래. 연락처를 드릴 테니까 직접 찾아가서 물어보시죠.

1000687089.english: If you figure out the secret, I wouldn't mind if you shared it with me... hehe.
1000687089.korean: 비법을 알게 되면 저한테 슬쩍 알려주셔도 좋고... 흐흐.

1000687091.english: Dutch Girl... from all the research so far, no matter how diligent an individual is, they always lose their work motivation within 5–6 years.
1000687091.korean: 더치걸... 여태까지 조사한 결과로는, 아무리 열성적인 개체라고 해도 5~6년 안에 근로 의욕을 상실하던데.

1000687092.english: A Dutch Girl that has maintained her state for over 7 years... that's intriguing.
1000687092.korean: 7년 넘게 원 상태를 유지하고 있는 더치걸이라... 흥미롭네.

1000687093.english: So... what could the secret be? I hope it's a fun story.
1000687093.korean: 그래서... 과연 어떤 비밀이 있을까. 재밌는 이야기면 좋겠네.

1000687094.english: Ah, hello, this is Dr. Miranda Lee from the PECS technical department. Is this a good time to talk? Yes, the reason I'm calling is...
1000687094.korean: 아, 안녕하세요, PECS 기술부의 미란다 리 박사라고 합니다. 혹시 통화 가능하신가요? 네, 다름이 아니고...

1000687095.english: Ah, hello. You must be Dr. Miranda Lee who reached out?
1000687095.korean: 아, 안녕하십니까. 연락 주신 미란다 리 박사님 맞으시죠?

1000687098.english: Then, Dr. Lee, please allow me to guide you to the office.

1000687099.english: So, the reason you've come is clearly... about Dutch Girl.
1000687099.korean: 그래서, 찾아오신 이유가 분명... 더치걸 때문이었죠.

1000687100.english: To be precise, about the Dutch Girl who, strangely enough, hasn't lost her motivation to work for an unusually long time.
1000687100.korean: 정확히는, 이상할 정도로 오랫동안 의욕을 잃지 않고 일하는 더치걸 때문이죠.

1000687101.english: Let me be direct. What exactly is your secret?
1000687101.korean: 단도직입적으로 묻겠습니다. 대체 그 비법이 뭔가요?

1000687103.english: I am, after all, a scholar. I'm not used to speaking in circles.
1000687103.korean: 아무래도 전 학자라서요. 빙빙 돌려 말하는 건 익숙하지 않거든요.

1000687104.english: The phenomenon of Dutch Girls slacking off after five years is common to every model.
1000687104.korean: 5년이 지난 더치걸이 보이는 태업 현상은 모든 모델에서 공통적으로 일어나는 현상이었습니다.

1000687105.english: Of course, one could solve it with chemical brainwashing procedures or by installing hypnosis modules, but introducing such extra processes made Dutch Girl's price an issue.
1000687105.korean: 물론, 화학적 세뇌 과정을 거치거나 최면 모듈을 설치해서 해결할 수 있는 일이지만, 그런 추가적인 공정을 도입하자니 더치걸의 가격이 문제가 되었죠.

1000687106.english: Naturally, the moment such complicated processes are added, the price jumps up.
1000687106.korean: 확실히, 그런 복잡한 과정이 끼어드는 순간 가격이 확 뛰게 되겠죠.

1000687107.english: And it can no longer be called economical.
1000687107.korean: 더 이상 경제적이라고 할 수도 없을 거고요.

1000687108.english: Exactly. That's why I very much want to know the method your company uses.
1000687108.korean: 네. 그래서, 사장님께서 쓰신 방법을 꼭 알고 싶은 겁니다.

1000687109.english: Haha, I see. Well then, I'll tell you.
1000687109.korean: 하하, 그렇군요. 뭐, 그럼 알려드리도록 하죠.

1000687110.english: Oh, you'll share it that easily?
1000687110.korean: 어, 그렇게 쉽게 알려주셔도 되는 건가요?

1000687111.english: Of course. It's not so difficult or great that I'd need to charge money or demand something in return.
1000687111.korean: 그럼요, 돈을 받거나 따로 뭘 요구할 정도로 어렵고 대단한 방법도 아닌걸요.

1000687112.english: My secret is actually very simple. It's to treat Dutch Girls humanely.
1000687112.korean: 제 비법은 사실 굉장히 간단합니다. 바로, 더치걸을 인도적으로 대하는 거죠.

1000687113.english: That's... not the answer I expected.

1000687114.english: Haha, but aren't Bioroids and humans more or less the same?
1000687114.korean: 하하, 사실 바이오로이드나 사람이나 다 비슷한 거 아니겠습니까.

1000687115.english: In our mine, we motivate Dutch Girls by rewarding them whenever they achieve above a certain performance standard.
1000687115.korean: 저희 광산에서는 일정 실적 이상을 달성한 더치걸에게 보상을 주는 방식으로 더치걸들의 근로 의욕을 고취시키고 있습니다.

1000687116.english: Specifically, we let them spend an entire day enjoying themselves at a theme park.
1000687116.korean: 바로 테마파크에서 하루 종일 즐거운 시간을 보낼 수 있게 해 주는 거죠.

1000687117.english: No matter how durable and loyal Dutch Girls are built to be, after 5 or 6 years of harsh labor in the mines, they get exhausted. That's why they resort to tricks and slack off.
1000687117.korean: 아무리 더치걸이 튼튼하고 충성스럽게 만들어졌다고 하더라도, 5년에서 6년 가까이 광산에서 가혹한 노동을 하다 보면 지치게 됩니다. 요령을 부리고 태업을 하는 것도 그 때문이죠.

1000687118.english: But if you present them with clear rewards and goals like this, they hardly ever shirk their duties.
1000687118.korean: 하지만 이렇게, 더치걸들에게 명확한 보상과 목표를 제시해 동기부여를 한다면 어지간해서는 태업을 하지 않더군요.

1000687119.english: So in our mine, we give Dutch Girls vacations to the theme park, ensure they work within legal labor hours, and treat them the same as people.
1000687119.korean: 그래서 저희 광산에서는 더치걸에게 테마파크로 휴가를 보내주고, 법정근로시간을 지켜 근무를 시키는 등 사람과 동일한 대우를 하고 있습니다.

1000687121.english: Of course not. It's not like they're drawing salaries like humans. We can easily afford this much.
1000687121.korean: 물론이죠. 사람처럼 봉급을 지급받는 게 아니니까요. 이 정도 돈이야 얼마든지 쓸 수 있죠.

1000687122.english: You still look unconvinced. Then please, see for yourself.
1000687122.korean: 아무래도 못 믿으시는 표정이군요. 그럼 직접 보시죠.

1000687123.english: This is Dutch Girl No. 809, who produces the highest volume of ore in our mine.
1000687123.korean: 저희 광산에서 가장 많은 양의 광물을 채굴하는 809번 더치걸입니다.

1000687126.english: Take a break, it's rest time.

1000687127.english: No. To hit this month's mining quota, I need to work a bit more.
1000687127.korean: 안 돼. 이번 달 채굴량 기준선을 달성하려면 좀 더 일해야 해.

1000687128.english: Go rest first. I'll join you after I work a little longer.
1000687128.korean: 너 먼저 가서 쉬고 있어. 난 좀 더 작업하다가 갈게.

1000687129.english: ...How can I just leave you alone? I'll help, then we'll rest together.
1000687129.korean: ...너 혼자 두고 어떻게 그래. 좀 도와줄 테니까, 같이 쉬러 가자.

1000687130.english: ...Alright. Then please load the ones I mined into the cart.
1000687130.korean: ...그래. 그럼, 내가 캔 것들을 카트에 옮겨 담아줘.

1000687131.english: Since you helped, you can take a third of it.

1000687132.english: Haha, isn't it touching how they look out for one another?
1000687132.korean: 하하, 동형기끼리 챙겨주는 마음이 아름답지 않습니까?

1000687134.english: Ah, that's the performance standard I was telling you about that the Dutch Girls have to meet.
1000687134.korean: 아, 아까 말씀드렸던, 더치걸들이 달성해야 하는 실적입니다.

1000687135.english: If it were up to me, I'd send all of them to the theme park, but unfortunately that wouldn't serve as motivation. So we set a quota.
1000687135.korean: 마음 같아서는 모든 더치걸을 테마파크에 보내주고 싶지만, 안타깝게도 그러면 동기부여가 되지 않겠죠? 그래서 채굴량 기준선을 설정한 겁니다.

1000687136.english: And here is where the true nature of Bioroids shines.

1000687137.english: Humans aren't actually very rational creatures. As a result, when placed in competition, instead of improving their own efficiency, they sometimes drag others down to match their own level.
1000687137.korean: 사람은 사실 그렇게 합리적인 동물이 아닙니다. 그 결과, 지금처럼 경쟁 관계에 놓이면 자신의 능률을 높이기보단 남을 끌어내려 자신의 수준에 맞추려고 하는 일이 생길 때도 있죠.

1000687138.english: Competition often gets overheated, and there are cases where they end up fighting and biting each other.
1000687138.korean: 경쟁이 과열되어 서로 싸우고 물어뜯는 일이 생기는 경우가 종종 발생한단 말입니다.

1000687140.english: - But among Bioroids there is that... unique sisterhood, isn't there?
1000687140.korean: 하지만 바이오로이드간에는 그... 특유의 자매애가 있지 않습니까?

1000687141.english: - Not every model, but generally yes.
1000687141.korean: 안 그런 모델도 있지만, 뭐 대체로는 그렇죠.

1000687142.english: - Thanks to that, even when competition overheats, Bioroids generally don't try to pull others down. Didn't you just see? Number 854 helping number 809.
1000687142.korean: 그 덕분에 바이오로이드는 경쟁이 과열되더라도, 어지간해서는 남을 끌어내리려 하지 않습니다. 방금도 보시지 않았습니까? 854번 더치걸이 809번 더치걸을 돕는 모습을.

1000687143.english: - That's the secret to why our Dutch Girls work for so long at our mine. Dignified treatment and reasonable competition.
1000687143.korean: 그게 바로 저희 광산에서 더치걸이 오랫동안 일하는 비법입니다. 인격적인 대우와 합리적인 경쟁.

1000687144.english: Hmm... it's a good method, but hard to apply in a lab Setting.
1000687144.korean: 흐음... 좋은 방법이지만, 실험실에서 써먹기는 힘들 것 같네요.

1000687146.english: Couldn't the theme park get boring?

1000687147.english: There is a threshold for stimuli. No matter how tasty a food is, if you eat it every day you get tired of it; likewise, if you keep applying a stimulus, you eventually become desensitized to it.
1000687147.korean: 자극에는 역치라는 게 있습니다. 아무리 맛있는 음식이라고 해도 매일 먹다 보면 질리는 것처럼, 자극을 가하다 보면 결국 그 자극에 무감각해지기 마련이죠.

1000687148.english: If the Dutch Girls grow tired of the theme park, what then?
1000687148.korean: 만약 더치걸들이 테마파크에 질리게 된다면, 그 때는 어떻게 되는 거죠?

1000687149.english: - Good point. Well, the method is actually very simple.
1000687149.korean: 좋은 지적이십니다. 뭐, 그 방법은 사실 굉장히 간단하죠.

1000687150.english: - Make it so they don't get tired of it.

1000687151.english: Phew, done... let's go back now.

1000687152.english: Thanks, 854. Because of you, I filled today's quota.
1000687152.korean: 고마워, 854번. 너 덕분에 오늘 할 양은 채웠네.

1000687153.english: But you already met today's quota earlier...

1000687154.english: Not enough to go to the theme park.
1000687154.korean: 테마파크에 가기 위해 필요한 만큼은 아니었어.

1000687155.english: Theme parks are great, but first we have to live and eat...

1000687156.english: ...Come to think of it, I am hungry. The canteen must be closed...
1000687156.korean: ...그러고 보니 배고프긴 하네. 식당은 문을 닫았을 테고...

1000687157.english: Sigh, I thought so. Here, it's stale bread, but with water it's passable.
1000687157.korean: 에휴, 그럴 것 같더라. 자, 여기. 말라비틀어진 빵이지만, 물이랑 같이 먹으면 그럭저럭 괜찮아.

1000687159.english: I figured you'd likely skip dinner to keep working, so I brought it.
1000687159.korean: 보나마나 밥도 안 먹고 일할 것 같아서 챙겨 놨어.

1000687161.english: I wonder how wonderful the theme park must be for you to behave like this... take care of yourself, even in self-management.
1000687161.korean: 테마파크가 얼마나 재밌었길래 그러는지... 자기관리에도 신경 써가면서 좀 해.

1000687162.english: I don't understand why someone who's been at the mine 8 years still works like a rookie, starving themselves...
1000687162.korean: 광산에서 일한지도 8년이나 되는 애가, 왜 아직도 초년차처럼 밥도 굶어가며 일하는지 모르겠네...

1000687163.english: Wait, she's been working diligently even after 8 years?
1000687163.korean: 잠깐, 8년이나 지났는데도 저렇게 열심히 일을 했다고요?

1000687164.english: Yes. Thanks to the fantasy of the theme park.

1000687165.english: Actually, as you know... the theme park isn't some miraculous paradise. At best it's a place to ride a few attractions.
1000687165.korean: 사실, 이미 잘 아시겠지만... 테마파크가 그렇게 엄청나게 환상적인 곳은 아니죠. 기껏해야 놀이기구나 좀 타는 곳 아닙니까?

1000687166.english: Yet we long for the theme park like longing for a first love. We keep repainting past experiences in our heads, turning them into something grander than they were.
1000687166.korean: 그럼에도 우리가 테마파크에 가고 싶어하는 건, 마치 첫사랑에 대한 동경과 비슷한 겁니다. 과거의 경험을 머릿속에서 계속 덧칠해서, 실제보다 더 멋진 뭔가로 바꿔 놓는 거죠.

1000687167.english: If you met your first love again now, would they look as beautiful as you remembered? No. You'd be pretty disappointed.
1000687167.korean: 첫사랑을 지금 와서 다시 만난다면 어떨 것 같습니까? 내가 기억하던 그 모습처럼 아름다울까요? 아니죠. 상당히 실망스러울 겁니다.

1000687168.english: The fantasy of the theme park that motivates Dutch Girls is the same. If Dutch Girl 809 actually went again now, she'd be disappointed by how the reality differs from her memory.
1000687168.korean: 더치걸들에게 동기를 주는 테마파크에 대한 환상도 마찬가지입니다. 809번 더치걸이 지금 와서 다시 테마파크에 간다면, 분명 자신의 기억과는 다른 모습에 실망하게 될 겁니다.

1000687169.english: That's why the key is to set the mining quota just barely beyond what the Dutch Girl's physical ability can achieve.
1000687169.korean: 그래서 채굴량 기준선을 아슬아슬하게 더치걸의 신체 능력으로 채굴할 수 없는 수준으로 설정하는 것이 핵심인 겁니다.

1000687170.english: Heh, interesting. But didn't you say humane treatment is the core?
1000687170.korean: 후훗, 그거 참 재밌네요. 인도적인 대우가 핵심이라고 하지 않으셨나요?

1000687171.english: Haha, I didn't lie. We used a similar method when hiring people.
1000687171.korean: 하하, 거짓말은 안 했습니다. 사람들 고용했을 때에도 비슷한 방법을 썼으니까요.

1000687172.english: What if the Dutch Girls realize the quota is an unattainable goal?
1000687172.korean: 만약 더치걸들이 채굴량 기준선이 자기들이 닿을 수 없는 목표라는 사실을 깨닫는다면요?

1000687173.english: Then sometimes we lower the quota so they can visit the theme park once.
1000687173.korean: 그래서 가끔은 채굴량 기준선을 낮춰서, 테마파크에 한 번 방문할 수 있게 해 주는 거죠.

1000687175.english: If it's false hope, so what? If someone in hell is given a ladder thinking it's a path to heaven... whether that ladder is rope or a thin spiderweb doesn't matter.
1000687175.korean: 가짜 희망이면 어떻습니까? 지옥에 떨어진 사람에게 천국으로 가는 줄사다리가 내려온다면... 사다리가 밧줄로 만들어져 있는지, 얇은 거미줄로 만들어져 있는지는 중요하지 않은 법이죠.

1000687176.english: What matters is that heaven is spread above their heads and a way out of hell is presented in front of them.
1000687176.korean: 중요한 건 머리 위에 천국이 펼쳐져 있고, 이 지옥을 빠져나갈 방법이 눈 앞에 제시되어 있다는 겁니다.

1000687177.english: So they cling desperately to that ladder.
1000687177.korean: 그래서 그 사다리에 저렇게 필사적으로 매달리게 되는 거죠.

1000687178.english: That sounds like the life of someone addicted to gambling or drugs.
1000687178.korean: 그 부분만 들어서는 도박이나 마약에 중독된 사람들의 삶처럼 들리는군요.

1000687180.english: People and Bioroids are pretty similar, aren't they?
1000687180.korean: 사실 바이오로이드나 사람이나 다 비슷한 거 아니겠습니까.

1000687181.english: So... is the secret twofold? False hope and deception policy.
1000687181.korean: 그래서... 결국 비법은 두 가지인가요? 가짜 희망과 기만 정책.

1000687182.english: It's plausible, but what if a Dutch Girl performs exceptionally and visits the theme park many times?
1000687182.korean: 괜찮기는 하지만... 만약 더치걸 하나가 뛰어난 퍼포먼스를 발휘해서 여러 번 테마파크를 가게 된다면요?

1000687183.english: Sometimes a Bioroid exceeds its design performance.
1000687183.korean: 간혹 설계 이상의 퍼포먼스를 보이는 바이오로이드가 있잖아요.

1000687184.english: Also, if Dutch Girls concertedly coordinate so one individual can visit the theme park multiple times... there are lots of loopholes.
1000687184.korean: 그리고 또, 더치걸들이 작정하고 단합해서 한 개체가 여러 번 테마파크에 들르는 경우도 있을 테고... 이래저래 허점이 많이 보이는군요.

1000687185.english: Haha, well, it's not a perfect method.
1000687185.korean: 하하, 뭐 완벽한 방법이라고 할 수는 없죠.

1000687186.english: Didn't I say it wasn't difficult or grand, so I was fine sharing it?
1000687186.korean: 제가 말씀드리지 않았습니까? 어렵고 대단한 방법이 아니라서 공유해도 괜찮다고.

1000687187.english: Still, I have a solution for one of those two problems.
1000687187.korean: 그래도 두 가지 문제 중 하나에 대한 해결책은 가지고 있습니다.

1000687189.english: As you suggested, to prevent one Dutch Girl from visiting the theme park repeatedly and breaking the fantasy...
1000687189.korean: 말씀하신 대로, 더치걸 하나가 여러 번 테마파크를 방문해 테마파크에 대한 환상이 깨지는 것을 막기 위해서...

1000687190.english: The vehicle visiting the theme park for the second time will not head to Area A.
1000687190.korean: 두 번째로 테마파크에 방문하는 차량은 A구역으로 향하지 않을 겁니다.

1000687192.english: - A contradictory statement, but... at least for me it's true.
1000687192.korean: - 모순적인 말이지만... 적어도, 내게 있어서는 그렇다.

1000687193.english: - Every night I leave my small cave lodging and head for the theme park.
1000687193.korean: - 매일 밤마다 나는 동굴 속의 내 작은 숙소를 벗어나 테마파크로 향한다.

1000687194.english: - The warm sunlight wrapping my body. The wind playfully messing my hair. Children's laughs clear as glass, and hands offering cloudlike sweet, fluffy cotton candy...
1000687194.korean: - 따스하게 몸을 감싸는 햇살. 짓궂게 머리카락을 헤집는 바람. 유리처럼 투명한 아이들의 웃음소리와, 구름처럼 달콤하고 폭신한 솜사탕을 건네는 손길...

1000687195.english: - I know it's a dream, but the sight makes me smile nonetheless.
1000687195.korean: - 꿈이라는 걸 알지만, 그럼에도 절로 미소가 지어지는 광경이다.

1000687196.english: - A sensation I would want to feel for a lifetime, but sadly I cannot. Dreams exist to be woken from.
1000687196.korean: - 평생 느끼고 싶은 감각이지만, 안타깝게도 그럴 수는 없다. 꿈이라는 건 본래 깨어나기 위해 존재하기에.

1000687197.english: Wake up! Wake up! It's work time!

1000687198.english: - Five in the morning. The loud knocking on the door and a sister calling wake-up time.
1000687198.korean: - 아침 다섯 시. 방 문을 두드리는 깡깡 소리와 함께, 자매가 기상 시간을 알려온다.

1000687199.english: - Time to return to cold reality.

1000687200.english: - Flickering light about to go out. A mattress smelling of mold from lack of sunlight and washing. A wall that sheds stone dust when tapped because it's cracked.
1000687200.korean: - 금방이라도 꺼질 듯이 깜빡이는 전등. 오랫동안 빨지도, 햇빛을 받지도 않아 곰팡이 냄새가 진동하는 매트리스. 금이 가서 툭 두드리면 돌가루가 떨어지는 벽.

1000687201.english: - This is the scene I face every morning.
1000687201.korean: - 이게 매일 아침 내가 마주하는 풍경이다.

1000687203.english: Yeah. Get up. We have work to do.

1000687206.english: Really? Is it the boss's birthday?

1000687207.english: I think they said earlier this month that the company's situation was bad, so they were cutting rations…
1000687207.korean: 전에 이번 달에는 회사 사정이 안 좋아서 배급 줄인다고 했던 것 같은데...

1000687208.english: Yeah, it's black bread and cabbage soup with salted fish.
1000687208.korean: 맞아, 흑빵이랑 염장 생선이 들어간 양배추 수프야.

1000687210.english: …Soup, my ass. It's just water they dunked fish in…

1000687211.english: Still, it's better than when they used to serve fried beans.
1000687211.korean: 그래도 콩 튀김이 나오던 때보다는 낫겠지.

1000687212.english: Ugh, don't even mention fried beans. Horrible.
1000687212.korean: 으으, 콩 튀김 얘기 하지 마. 끔찍해.

1000687213.english: But the boss says our situation is actually better. In the mine next door, they're given bread made from turnips and sawdust.
1000687213.korean: 그래도 사장이 그러는데, 우리는 상황이 나은 편이래. 옆 광산에서는 순무랑 톱밥으로 만든 빵을 준다는 거야.

1000687214.english: Can you believe it? Turnips are one thing, but making bread out of sawdust…
1000687214.korean: 믿어져? 순무는 그렇다 쳐도, 톱밥으로 빵을 만든다니...

1000687215.english: Good thing we don't live at the neighboring mine.
1000687215.korean: 우리가 옆 광산에 사는 게 아니라 다행이다.

1000687217.english: Next month I'll really work hard and go to that theme park too. When I get there, I'll eat that… cotton candy you mentioned, churros, and try all the delicious food.
1000687217.korean: 다음 달에는 진짜 열심히 해서 나도 그 테마파크에 가봐야지. 테마파크에 가면 네가 말했던 그... 솜사탕도 먹고, 츄로스도 먹고, 맛있는 건 다 먹어볼 거야.

1000687218.english: Remember this, next month I'll be the one who takes first place.
1000687218.korean: 기억해 둬, 다음 달 1등은 내가 할 거니까.

1000687220.english: Let's go. We need to get our rations and head into the mine.
1000687220.korean: 이제 가자. 배식 받고 광산으로 들어가야지.

1000687221.english: The life of a Dutch Girl is, for the most part, very simple.

1000687222.english: When the door opens, everyone lines up in rows and heads toward the mine.
1000687222.korean: - 방 문이 열리면, 다 같이 오와 열을 맞춰 광산으로 향한다.

1000687223.english: Along the way, they receive bread from the meal-duty Dutch Girl, wrap it carefully in cloth, and put the fish soup into a container, making sure to close the lid tightly so that dust and stone powder don't get in.
1000687223.korean: - 중간에 빵을 나눠주는 식사 당번 더치걸에게 빵을 받아 천으로 잘 감싸고, 생선 수프는 통에 담아 먼지와 돌가루가 들어가지 않게 뚜껑을 잘 닫는다.

1000687224.english: Wow, look at this! There's actually quite a lot of fish meat in it!
1000687224.korean: 와, 이것 봐! 생선 살이 꽤 많이 들어있어!

1000687225.english: I'll give you half. Let's share it later.
1000687225.korean: 반 줄게. 이따가 같이 나눠 먹자.

1000687226.english: And then they line up and head toward the mine.
1000687226.korean: - 그리고 나서 이제 줄을 서서 광산으로 향하는 것이다.

1000687227.english: The new Dutch Girls eat their bread as soon as they receive it, since last night's rations were too small.
1000687227.korean: - 신입 더치걸들은 빵을 받자마자 먹어 치운다. 어젯밤 배식량이 적었던 탓이다.

1000687228.english: But filling their stomachs with the morning rations is nearly impossible. In the end, the new Dutch Girls must clutch their hungry bellies and endure hard labor all day long.
1000687228.korean: - 하지만 아침에 받은 배식량으로 배를 채우는 건 불가능에 가깝다. 결국, 신입들은 하루 종일 굶주린 배를 움켜쥐고 힘든 노동을 해야 하는 것이다.

1000687229.english: Come on… don't eat it now, save it and eat together when the lunch rations come…
1000687229.korean: 참... 지금 먹지 말고, 아껴 뒀다가 이따가 점심 배식 때 같이 먹으라니까...

1000687230.english: Leave them be. They'll learn soon enough.

1000687231.english: Still, it hurts to see them so hungry.
1000687231.korean: 그래도 배고파 하는 거 보면 마음 아프잖아.

1000687232.english: We all went through the same thing.

1000687233.english: We're here. Let's get to work.

1000687234.english: Under the dim lights, they dig into the ground with pickaxes and drills. They break the bedrock, brace it so it won't collapse, then break more rock and brace it again.
1000687234.korean: - 어두운 조명 아래에서, 곡괭이와 드릴로 땅을 판다. 암반을 부수고, 그 자리에 무너지지 않게 받침대를 덧댄다. 다시 암반을 부수고, 받침대를 덧댄다.

1000687235.english: The broken rocks are dumped into a nearby pit, and if minerals are found, they're loaded onto a cart for collection.
1000687235.korean: - 부순 바위들은 근처 구덩이에 쏟아 붓고, 광물이 보이면 카트에 실어 회수한다.

1000687236.english: Just as their hands gripping the pickaxes and drills start to go numb, the sound signaling lunch break is heard.
1000687236.korean: - 곡괭이와 드릴을 쥔 손이 얼얼해질 때쯤, 슬슬 점심 휴식시간을 알리는 소리가 들린다.

1000687237.english: Ah, finally it's over. Let's go get our ration of nutrient porridge.
1000687237.korean: 아, 드디어 끝났네. 영양죽 배급받고 오자.

1000687240.english: You're planning to mine even during lunch again, aren't you? At this rate you'll collapse for real.
1000687240.korean: 또 점심시간에도 광물 캐려고 그러지? 그러다 진짜 쓰러진다.

1000687241.english: Even if Bioroids are tough, it doesn't mean they don't feel pain.
1000687241.korean: 바이오로이드가 튼튼하긴 해도, 아프지 않은 건 아니란 말이야.

1000687242.english: If you work for too long, you'll get blisters on your hands, and sometimes your hands split open from swinging the pickaxe… What will you do if you collapse from skipping meals on top of that?
1000687242.korean: 오랫동안 일하면 손에 물집도 잡히고, 곡괭이질 하다가 손이 터지기도 하는데... 밥까지 굶어가면서 그러다가 쓰러지면 어쩌려고 그래.

1000687243.english: If you collapse, don't expect me to carry you, okay?
1000687243.korean: 너 쓰러지면 난 안 업고 갈 거야?

1000687246.english: Fine. I'll go get the lunch rations then.
1000687246.korean: 그래. 그럼 난 점심 배급 받고 올게.

1000687247.english: They quickly shove the food they received into their mouths and pick up the drill again.
1000687247.korean: - 배식 받은 음식들을 대충 입에 털어넣고, 다시 드릴을 집어든다.

1000687248.english: They have to start working again as fast as possible.
1000687248.korean: - 최대한 빠르게 다시 작업을 시작해야 한다.

1000687249.english: They didn't want to make anyone worry, but there was no choice. There's simply no way to meet the quota through normal means.
1000687249.korean: - 걱정시키기는 싫었지만, 어쩔 수 없다. 정상적인 방법으로는 절대로 채굴량을 맞출 수가 없으니까.

1000687250.english: They hated to admit it, but the boss was a truly clever man. Every time, he set the quota just barely out of reach, no matter how hard they tried.
1000687250.korean: - 인정하긴 싫지만, 사장은 정말 똑똑한 인간이다. 매번 우리가 아무리 노력해도 아슬아슬하게 닿지 않을 정도로 채굴량을 결정하니까.

1000687251.english: A few of the other Dutch Girls seem to have realized it as well. But we silently agreed never to speak of it.
1000687251.korean: - 다른 더치걸 몇몇도 그 사실을 알아차린 것 같다. 하지만, 우리는 암묵적으로 그 사실을 입 밖으로 내지 않기로 합의했다.

1000687252.english: Because the moment we said it out loud, we were too afraid of how the other Dutch Girls might react.
1000687252.korean: - 그 사실을 입 밖으로 내놓는 순간, 다른 더치걸들의 반응이 너무나도 두렵기에.

1000687253.english: And because it felt like even the little hope we had left would vanish.
1000687253.korean: - 그리고 우리에게 남아있는 희망조차 사라질 것 같기에.

1000687254.english: That's why there's only one thing we can do.
1000687254.korean: - 그렇기에 우리가 할 수 있는 일은 하나뿐이다.

1000687255.english: Grip the drill, drive it into the rock, and switch it on.
1000687255.korean: - 드릴을 들고, 암반에 박아 넣은 뒤 시동을 켜는 것.

1000687256.english: Still, this time I was lucky. I was able to mine far more minerals than I expected.
1000687256.korean: - 그래도 이번에는 운이 좋았다. 내가 생각한 것보다 꽤 많은 광물을 캘 수 있었으니까.

1000687257.english: There are still about three days left until this month's tally. If luck stays on my side, this month I'll definitely be able to go to the theme park. And if that happens…
1000687257.korean: - 아직 이번 달 정산까지는 사흘 정도 남았다. 이대로 운이 따라준다면, 이번 달에는 분명 테마파크에 갈 수 있을 것이다. 그렇다면...

1000687260.english: My body feels heavy, like I'm carrying a load of sand. My consciousness grows dim, and I can feel the ground rushing up toward my face.
1000687260.korean: - 마치 모래를 잔뜩 짊어진 것처럼 몸이 무겁다. 의식이 점차 흐려지고, 땅바닥이 얼굴로 다가오는 것이 느껴진다.

1000687262.english: I have to tell 854… tell the others…

1000687266.english: I told you, you'd collapse if you kept that up…

1000687267.english: …Didn't you say you wouldn't carry me?

1000687268.english: How could I just leave behind someone as stubborn as you? If I did, you'd wake up and go right back to digging.
1000687268.korean: 너처럼 미련한 애를 어떻게 내버려 두겠어. 기절했다가 정신차리면 다시 땅 파러 갈 텐데.

1000687269.english: …That was supposed to be a joke.

1000687271.english: Did you really want it that badly?

1000687273.english: The theme park, I mean. Just how amazing could it be that you're so obsessed with it?
1000687273.korean: 테마파크 말이야. 대체 거기가 얼마나 좋았으면 그렇게 집착하는 거야?

1000687274.english: You don't have the strength to move right now anyway, so tell me about it.
1000687274.korean: 어차피 지금은 움직일 힘도 없을 텐데, 그 얘기나 좀 해 봐.

1000687276.english: The theme park… of course it was fun.

1000687277.english: But that wasn't what I truly wanted.
1000687277.korean: 하지만 내가 원하던 건 그런 게 아니야.

1000687278.english: The sunlight and the breeze, the flowers in full bloom, the well-kept gardens…
1000687278.korean: 햇빛과 바람, 아름답게 핀 꽃과 잘 가꿔진 정원...

1000687279.english: And more than anything, the sight of someone smiling at me… I loved that so much.
1000687279.korean: 그리고, 무엇보다도, 내게 미소를 지어주던 모습이... 그 모습이 너무 좋았어.

1000687280.english: …Things you could never experience down here underground.

1000687282.english: I wanted to share that feeling, those emotions, with the other Dutch Girls too.
1000687282.korean: 이 느낌을, 그때의 감정을 다른 더치걸들에게도 말해주고 싶었어.

1000687283.english: I wanted to tell them that above our heads there are so many beautiful things.
1000687283.korean: 우리 머리 위에는 이렇게나 아름다운 것들이 많다는 사실을 설명해주고 싶었는데...

1000687284.english: But I couldn't explain it. I'd never received that kind of warmth before…
1000687284.korean: 설명할 수가 없었어. 그런 따뜻한 손길을 받아본 적이 없었거든...

1000687285.english: That's why I wanted to go to the theme park once more, to find a way to explain that feeling.
1000687285.korean: 그래서, 다시 한 번 테마파크에 가서 그 느낌을 설명할 방법을 찾아보고 싶었던 거야.

1000687288.english: Haa, I feel like I've recovered a bit of strength…
1000687288.korean: 후우, 체력이 좀 회복된 것 같네..

1000687290.english: Back to mining, of course… I can't just sit around like this.
1000687290.korean: 채굴하러 가야지... 이렇게 쉬고 있으면 안 돼.

1000687291.english: This month's quota was set pretty low, so if I just push a little harder, I'll be able to go to the theme park.
1000687291.korean: 이번 달 채굴량 기준선은 꽤 낮게 잡혔으니까, 조금만 더 열심히 하면 테마파크에 갈 수 있을 거야.

1000687292.english: This time, I'll definitely find a way to explain that feeling…
1000687292.korean: 이번에는 꼭, 그 느낌을 설명할 방법을...

1000687295.english: Don't push yourself. You're not fully recovered yet…
1000687295.korean: 무리하지 마. 다 나은 게 아니니까...

1000687298.english: …Sigh. Honestly, how could anyone stop a stubborn mule like you.
1000687298.korean: ...에휴. 그래, 너 같은 고집쟁이를 어떻게 말리겠니.

1000687304.english: - 854 pulled her own cart over and dumped all the ore inside into 809's cart.
1000687304.korean: - 854번은 자기 카트를 끌고 오더니, 그 안에 있던 광석들을 전부 809번의 카트에 쏟아부었다.

1000687306.english: Helping you get to the theme park.
1000687306.korean: 테마파크에 갈 수 있게 도와주는 거지.

1000687307.english: You said that if you went again, you might finally discover what that feeling really was.
1000687307.korean: 다시 한 번 가면 그 느낌의 정체를 알 수 있을 것 같다며.

1000687308.english: That was… I only said maybe I'd find out this time…
1000687308.korean: 그건... 이번에는 그 느낌을 알 수 있을지도 모른다는 말이었어...

1000687309.english: And what about you? You've never even been to the theme park.
1000687309.korean: 그리고... 넌? 넌 한 번도 테마파크에 간 적이 없잖아.

1000687310.english: You should be the one going, not me…!

1000687311.english: I was nowhere near the quota anyway.
1000687311.korean: 어차피 난 채굴량 기준선에 훨씬 못 미쳤어.

1000687312.english: And even if I did go, I'd probably be the same as you—unable to properly explain the feeling I had there.
1000687312.korean: 그리고 내가 간다고 해도, 나도 너랑 비슷했을 걸. 거기서 겪었던 느낌을 제대로 설명하지 못했을 거야.

1000687313.english: So it's better if you're the one who goes.
1000687313.korean: 그럴 바에는 네가 가는 게 낫지.

1000687314.english: You… you're mistaken if you think I'm doing this for free.
1000687314.korean: 너, 뭐 착각하고 있는 것 같은데, 이거 공짜로 해주는 거 아니야.

1000687315.english: Then… what should I give you?

1000687316.english: I always really enjoyed your stories about the theme park.
1000687316.korean: 네가 해 주는 테마파크 얘기, 난 굉장히 좋아했거든.

1000687317.english: So this time, when you go, make sure you figure out what that feeling truly is.
1000687317.korean: 그러니까 이번에 테마파크에 가서, 꼭 그 느낌이 뭔지 알아내 봐.

1000687318.english: And next time, you'll have to tell me an even better story.
1000687318.korean: 그리고 이번에는 더 재밌는 얘기를 들려줘야 해.

1000687319.english: That's the price for me giving you my ore. Got it?
1000687319.korean: 그게 내가 광물을 넘겨주는 대가야. 알겠지?

1000687321.english: Yeah, I will…! I'll tell you stories far happier and better than last time…!
1000687321.korean: 응, 그럴게...! 저번보다 훨씬 즐겁고 좋은 얘기들을 들려줄게...!

1000687326.english: No, I can't just sit here. I'll have to go ask the boss…

1000687328.english: Oh, 854. It's you. What is it?

1000687329.english: Um, boss… it's about Dutch Girl 809, the one who visited the theme park today…
1000687329.korean: 저기, 사장님... 오늘 테마파크를 방문했던 809번 더치걸 말인데요...

1000687330.english: Ah, the one who shares a room with you? She's an excellent one—I know her well, since she's surpassed the mining quota twice.
1000687330.korean: 아, 너랑 같은 방을 쓰는 그 더치걸 말이지? 두 번이나 채굴량 기준선을 넘긴 우수한 녀석이라서, 내가 잘 알고 있지.

1000687331.english: …Right? You don't look so well. Am I mistaken?
1000687331.korean: ...맞지? 표정이 안 좋은데, 혹시 내가 착각한 거니?

1000687332.english: No, no. You're right. She's my roommate.
1000687332.korean: 아뇨, 아뇨. 맞아요. 저랑 같은 방을 쓰고 있어요.

1000687333.english: It's just… the visiting hours for the theme park should be over by now, but she still hasn't come back…
1000687333.korean: 테마파크 관람 시간은 끝난 걸로 아는데, 아직까지 안 돌아와서요...

1000687335.english: Ah, so that's what this is about…
