1000687336.english: Don't worry too much. 809 has… gone to a better place.
1000687336.korean: 너무 걱정하지 말거라. 809번은... 좋은 곳으로 갔으니까.

1000687337.english: As you know, 809 was an outstanding Bioroid who surpassed the mining quota twice.
1000687337.korean: 알다시피, 809번은 두 번이나 채굴량 기준선을 넘긴 우수한 바이오로이드잖니?

1000687338.english: To show her some gratitude, I gave her a choice.
1000687338.korean: 그런 809번에게 뭔가 감사의 의미를 표하고 싶어서, 809번에게 선택권을 줬단다.

1000687339.english: The right to decide whether to keep living in the theme park, or return to the mine and continue working.
1000687339.korean: 테마파크에서 계속 살 건지, 아니면 광산으로 돌아와서 일을 할 건지 선택할 수 있는 권리 말이야.

1000687340.english: So 809… chose to stay in the theme park?
1000687340.korean: 809번은... 테마파크에서 계속 사는 걸 선택한 건가요?

1000687341.english: That's right. Isn't that fortunate? She won't have to struggle with work anymore…
1000687341.korean: 그렇구나. 다행이네요. 이제 힘들게 일하지 않아도 될 테니까...

1000687343.english: Oh my, it's getting late. Go on and rest. You'll need to work again tomorrow.
1000687343.korean: 어이쿠, 시간이 늦었구나. 이만 들어가서 쉬렴. 내일 다시 일을 하러 가야지.

1000687346.english: Right, don't be gloomy. This is a good thing.
1000687346.korean: 그래, 우울해 하지 말자. 잘 된 일이잖아.

1000687347.english: It's a good thing… so why does it hurt so much?
1000687347.korean: 잘 된 일인데... 왜 이렇게 마음이 아프지.

1000687349.english: Why do you look so down?

1000687352.english: Huh? What about 809? Did she get hurt?

1000687353.english: No, she went to the theme park this time…

1000687354.english: Whoa, really? Lucky her. She must've worked really hard.
1000687354.korean: 와, 진짜? 부럽다. 엄청 열심히 일했나 보네.

1000687355.english: Yeah… she really did work hard.

1000687356.english: I heard today that she decided to stay and live there.
1000687356.korean: 오늘 들었는데, 걔가 거기서 살기로 했다는 거야.

1000687357.english: Even though she promised she'd come back and tell me all about the theme park.
1000687357.korean: 돌아와서 나한테 테마파크 얘기를 해 주기로 했으면서 말이야.

1000687358.english: But living in the theme park is better than working here, isn't it?
1000687358.korean: 근데 여기서 일하는 것보다는 테마파크에서 사는 게 더 좋은 일이잖아?

1000687359.english: So I'm torn… should I resent her for breaking our promise, or should I celebrate the good fortune that came her way?
1000687359.korean: 약속을 안 지킨 809번을 원망해야 할까, 아니면 809번에게 찾아온 행운을 축하해 줘야 할까... 고민하고 있었어.

1000687360.english: Mm, that is a tough one…

1000687361.english: But hey, she wasn't adopted off somewhere, she just chose to stay in the theme park, right?
1000687361.korean: 그래도, 어디로 입양된 게 아니라 테마파크에서 살기로 한 거지?

1000687362.english: Then you can still meet her if you go there.
1000687362.korean: 그러면 테마파크로 찾아가면 만날 수 있잖아.

1000687363.english: Just go to the theme park and tell her how you feel. Isn't that right?
1000687363.korean: 809번을 찾아가서 한 마디 해 주면 되지. 안 그래?

1000687364.english: …You're right. I should go find 809 and give her a proper scolding.
1000687364.korean: ...그러네. 내가 809번을 찾아가서 따끔하게 한 마디 해 줘야겠다.

1000687365.english: Thanks, I feel a little less gloomy now.
1000687365.korean: 고마워, 덕분에 우울한 게 좀 사라진 것 같아.

1000687366.english: No need to thank me for that.

1000687367.english: Alright then, let's both work hard again tomorrow! And don't get hurt!
1000687367.korean: 그럼, 내일도 힘내서 일하자! 다치지 말고!

1000687369.english: …Yeah, if 809 could pull it off, then I sure can too.

1000687370.english: Just you wait, 809… I'll make it to the theme park myself, and when I do, I'll give you a proper scolding!
1000687370.korean: 두고 봐, 809번... 나도 꼭 테마파크에 가서, 너한테 따끔하게 한 마디 해 줄 테니까!

1000687371.english: (A few days ago, the CCTV footage we found in the mine… so that's what was recorded.)
1000687371.korean: (며칠 전에 광산에서 찾은 CCTV 자료... 이런 이야기가 있었구나.)

1000687372.english: (The Commander probably had a good reason for not showing me.)
1000687372.korean: (사령관이 나한테 안 보여주려고 할 만 했네.)

1000687374.english: (It let me see how the other Dutch Girls lived before the fall…)
1000687374.korean: (멸망 전, 다른 더치걸들이 어떻게 살았는지 볼 수 있었으니까...)

1000687377.english: I can't just let this go.

1000687378.english: Whoa~ I didn't know the VR room had a feature like this.
1000687378.korean: 이야~ VR룸에 이런 기능이 있는 줄은 몰랐네요.

1000687379.english: Right? I only found out about it recently too.

1000687380.english: It really feels like all the fatigue from work just melts away…
1000687380.korean: 일해서 생긴 피로가 싹 풀리는 느낌이라 좋네요...

1000687381.english: Ah, I could totally take a nap like this.
1000687381.korean: 아, 이대로 낮잠 한 숨 자면 좋겠다.

1000687382.english: Still, it should be fine, right? It's not like we've got anything urgent going on.
1000687382.korean: 그래도 상관 없지 않겠어? 급한 일도 없고.

1000687383.english: The City Guard could raid this place at any moment. I don't know why, but lately they've been on edge…
1000687383.korean: 시티가드가 언제 이 현장을 급습할지 모른다고요. 이유는 잘 모르겠지만, 요즘 갑자기 신경이 곤두서 있어서...

1000687384.english: Yeah, it's their crackdown period right now, so it's definitely risky.

1000687385.english: Just yesterday, I almost got busted trying to sell moonshine on the sly.
1000687385.korean: 어제도 몰래 밀주를 팔다가 단속에 걸릴 뻔 했지 뭐예요.

1000687386.english: …Come on, it's pretty obvious the crackdown's because of that booze.
1000687386.korean: ...아무리 봐도 그 밀주 때문인 것 같은데?

1000687387.english: No waaay. I paid off the right people, so it's all taken care of.
1000687387.korean: 에이, 그럴리가요오. 제대로 뇌물을 주고 넘어갔다고요.

1000687388.english: Hey, that's still a real crime!

1000687389.english: Don't worry… moonshine only gets banned because cheap, low-quality liquor ends up flooding the market, right?
1000687389.korean: 걱정 마세요... 밀주는 품질 나쁜 술이 시중에 풀리기 때문에 금지되는 거잖아요?

1000687391.english: I only make booze that's good for your health, so it's fine~
1000687391.korean: 저는 건강에 좋은 술만 만드니까 괜찮아요~

1000687392.english: Hahaha, healthy liquor? Now you're starting to sound as ridiculous as that Combat Engineer Captain, Lumberjane.
1000687392.korean: 아하하, 건강에 좋은 술이라니. 그건 공병대대장 럼버제인처럼 말도 안 되는 소리잖아.

1000687393.english: But isn't Captain Lumberjane actually a Combat Engineer officer?

1000687394.english: No! I'm a lumberjack! I've got no business sitting in on commander meetings and the like!
1000687394.korean: 아니야! 난 벌목꾼이라고! 지휘관 회의 같은 곳에 낄 짬이 아니란 말이야!

1000687395.english: …Haah, whatever. I just want to rest for now.

1000687396.english: By the way, Circe, that swimsuit of yours is really bold.

1000687402.english: …If the City Guard catches you, won't that cause real trouble?
1000687402.korean: ...시티가드에 걸리면 진짜 문제 생기는 거 아니야?

1000687403.english: Ahaha, as if that would ever happen.

1000687404.english: We won't get caught, so it's fine.

1000687405.english: What are you, some kind of underworld big shot?

1000687408.english: Gasp… is it the City Guard?

1000687409.english: Circe, are you in there? It's me…

1000687410.english: Ah, Dutch Girl, it was you. Please, come in.

1000687412.english: I'm not sure what you mean. Hoho…
1000687412.korean: 무슨 말씀을 하시는지 잘 모르겠네요. 호호...

1000687413.english: Welcome, Dutch Girl. What brings you here?
1000687413.korean: 어서 오세요, 더치걸 님. 무슨 일이신가요?

1000687415.english: Of course not. You're always welcome here.

1000687416.english: So then, what brings you to me today?
1000687416.korean: 그래서, 오늘은 무슨 일로 절 찾아오셨을까요?

1000687417.english: Well… don't take this the wrong way, but listen.

1000687418.english: What do you think… about rebuilding the theme park?
1000687418.korean: 테마파크를... 다시 만드는 것에 대해 어떻게 생각해?

1000687420.english: You wouldn't bring something like this up without a reason, Dutch Girl. Could you tell me more in detail?
1000687420.korean: 더치걸 님이 아무 이유 없이 그런 얘기를 꺼내진 않겠죠. 자세한 이야기를 들어볼 수 있을까요?

1000687422.english: To be honest, I was a little worried about bringing this up. As you know, the theme park is a place that left many people wounded…
1000687422.korean: 사실, 이야기를 꺼내면서 좀 걱정을 하긴 했어. 알다시피, 테마파크라는 곳은 많은 사람에게 상처를 준 곳이잖아...

1000687423.english: And you, Circe, were hurt by it even more.

1000687424.english: But to someone, the theme park was also a name filled with hope.

1000687425.english: This is a video I found recently while investigating the nearby mining area with the Doctor.
1000687425.korean: 이건 최근 닥터와 함께 근처 광산 지역들을 조사하다가 찾은 영상이야.

1000687426.english: It contained the records of other Dutch Girls.

1000687427.english: The records showed that the Dutch Girls worked hard because they wanted to go to the theme park. It was because, in the theme park, there was someone who spoke warm words to them.
1000687427.korean: 그 기록에서, 더치걸들은 테마파크에 가기 위해 열심히 일을 했어. 왜냐면... 테마파크에서는 그 아이들에게 따뜻한 말을 건네는 사람이 있었거든.

1000687428.english: Because seeing someone smile at them and kindly reach out a hand brought them so much joy.
1000687428.korean: 걔들을 향해 미소를 지어주고, 친절하게 손을 내밀어주는 모습이 너무 기뻤기 때문이야.

1000687429.english: I... want to bring that theme park back to life.

1000687430.english: Not the B Zone or C Zone that catered to humanity's twisted desires. I want to rebuild the theme park that was joyful for everyone, as beautiful as a land from a fairy tale.
1000687430.korean: 인간들의 뒤틀린 욕망을 만족시키던 B구역이나 C구역이 아니야. 누구에게나 즐거운, 동화 속의 나라처럼 아름다웠던 테마파크를 다시 만들고 싶어.

1000687431.english: That's why I want to tell the Dutch Girl who appeared in this video: this is what a theme park is. This is what it should be.
1000687431.korean: 그래서, 이 영상에 나왔던 더치걸에게 알려주고 싶어. 테마파크는 이런 곳이라고. 이런 곳이어야 한다고.

1000687432.english: The place you dreamed of was this joyful.
1000687432.korean: 너희들이 꿈꿨던 곳은 이렇게 즐거운 곳이었다고.

1000687434.english: Alright. After hearing such a wonderful story, there's no way I could just sit still.
1000687434.korean: 좋아. 그런 멋진 이야기를 듣고서 가만히 있을 수 없지.

1000687435.english: I'll do my best to help too. If you ever need me, just say the word!
1000687435.korean: 나도 열심히 도와줄게. 혹시나 내 도움이 필요한 일이 있으면 언제든지 말만 해!

1000687437.english: Of course, building construction isn't exactly my specialty, but if you ever need wiring work or a communications network set up, just reach out to me!
1000687437.korean: 물론 건물 짓는 건 제 전문분야가 아니지만, 배선 작업이나 통신망 설치가 필요하다면 제게 연락해 주세요!

1000687438.english: ...Oh, come on, I haven't even decided on the style yet, so jumping ahead like that makes things difficult.
1000687438.korean: ...나 참, 아직 어떤 스타일로 만들지도 결정 안 했는데 벌써 그러면 곤란하죠.

1000687440.english: Of course. Even though I worked as a theme park guide and had some bad memories, not all of them were unpleasant.
1000687440.korean: 물론이죠. 테마파크 안내원 일을 하면서 나쁜 기억만 있던 건 아니거든요.

1000687441.english: Zone A was truly a joyful place. It was where children and parents held hands and enjoyed the rides we had worked so hard to maintain.
1000687441.korean: A구역은 정말 즐거운 곳이었어요. 아이들과 부모들이 손을 잡고, 저희가 열심히 관리한 놀이기구를 즐기던 곳이었죠.

1000687442.english: How could anyone oppose the idea of rebuilding such a place?
1000687442.korean: 그런 곳을 다시 만들자는 의견에 어떻게 반대할 수 있겠어요?

1000687443.english: The problem is deciding on what style to create it in.
1000687443.korean: 문제는 어떤 스타일로 만들지 결정을 해야 한다는 거죠.

1000687444.english: Well, now that Aqua Land already exists...

1000687445.english: It would be a problem if the concept overlapped.

1000687446.english: Of course… So, do you happen to have any good ideas?
1000687446.korean: 물론이죠... 그래서 말인데, 혹시 좋은 아이디어 있으신가요?

1000687447.english: Hmm, well, I'm not sure. I'll have to think about it.

1000687448.english: I do have a few ideas, though. For example, a witch-themed park…
1000687448.korean: 제가 몇 개 떠오르는 게 있긴 한데요. 예를 들면 마녀의 테마파크라던가...

1000687449.english: Isn't that a bit too focused on Lady Circe's personal tastes?
1000687449.korean: 너무 키르케님 취향만 잔뜩 들어가 있는 거 아니예요?

1000687450.english: We could make giant statues of the Commander and me.
1000687450.korean: 사령관님이랑 제 동상을 큼지막하게 만드는 거죠.

1000687451.english: No, that concept got rejected already.

1000687452.english: How about a "Network Park" concept instead…

1000687453.english: Whoa, what is this? Is everyone just trying to satisfy their own whims?
1000687453.korean: 어어, 뭐야. 다들 사심 채우는 자리야?

1000687456.english: If that's the case, let's just include everything.
1000687456.korean: 그럴 거면 그냥 다 넣는 걸로 하자.

1000687458.english: Yeah. Network Park, Witch Park, Lumberyard Park—all of them…
1000687458.korean: 네트워크 파크도, 마녀 파크도, 벌목장 파크도 다 넣어서...

1000687459.english: We'll make a theme park for everyone in Orca.
1000687459.korean: 오르카에 있는 모든 사람들을 위한 테마파크를 만드는 거야.

1000687462.english: Exactly… it really is a good idea.

1000687463.english: So, I guess that means the theme park's name is decided?

1000687464.english: Do you have any good ideas?

1000687465.english: You just said it, Dutch Girl—a theme park for everyone in Orca.
1000687465.korean: 방금 더치걸 님이 말씀하셨잖아요? 오르카에 있는 모든 사람들을 위한 테마파크라고.

1000687466.english: Then there's only one name that fits that concept.
1000687466.korean: 그럼 그 컨셉에 어울리는 이름은 하나밖에 없죠.

1000687468.english: I thought I'd be abandoned. Everyone dislikes gloomy, dark girls, after all. And yet... even seeing nuna looking so down, the Commander still likes her? Whether nuna is all worked up and excited, or completely sunk low... you love her just the same? Thank you. And... I love you.
1000687468.korean: 버려질 거라고 생각했어. 우울하고 음침한 여자애는 다들 싫어하니까. 그런데도... 누나가 우울해진 모습을 보고도, 사령관은 누나를 좋아해 주는구나? 잔뜩 들뜬 누나도, 한껏 가라앉은 누나도... 똑같이 사랑해 주는 거구나? 고마워. 그리고... 사랑해.

1000687471.english: Oh? You want to hear my story?

1000687472.english: Hmm, well, getting to know each other better always deepens our friendship.
1000687472.korean: 음음, 서로에 대해서 잘 알면 친분이 깊어지는 법이지.

1000687474.english: If I start from the First Allied War, one night wouldn't be enough…
1000687474.korean: 1차 연합전쟁 때부터 이야기를 하면 하룻밤으로는 부족할 테니…

1000687475.english: Hmm, I should start with when I first joined the Sky Knights.
1000687475.korean: 음, 내가 스카이 나이츠에 처음 전입했을 때의 이야기부터 해야겠군.

1000687476.english: So… after the Second Allied War was forcibly ended… the War of Ruin? That's what they call it?
1000687476.korean: 그러니까… 2차 연합전쟁이 강제로 끝나고...... 멸망 전쟁? 그 전쟁을 멸망 전쟁이라고 부르는군?

1000687477.english: Anyway, this story takes place shortly after the War of Ruin began.
1000687477.korean: 어쨌든. 멸망 전쟁이 시작되고 얼마 지나지 않았을 때의 이야기일세.

1000687481.english: I asked if we've arrived at the destination.

1000687483.english: You said something similar earlier, didn't you…
1000687483.korean: 아까 전에도 비슷한 이야기를 한 거 같은데…

1000687484.english: If we flew, we'd probably be there already, right?
1000687484.korean: 날아서 왔으면 이미 도착했을 것 같네만?

1000687485.english: Do you want to let the Metal Parasites "catch and eat me"?

1000687486.english: If I go alone, I can dodge them and arrive just fine.
1000687486.korean: 나 혼자라면 충분히 피해서 도착할 수 있다네.

1000687488.english: When pleasure is quantified, isn't it better for only one person to struggle rather than both?
1000687488.korean: 쾌락을 수량화 했을 때 두 명 다 힘든 것보다, 한 명만 힘들어지는 것이 좋지 않나?

1000687489.english: Then even if you fly, it wouldn't make much difference.
1000687489.korean: 그러면 네가 날아가도 별 차이 없을 것 같은데.

1000687493.english: If you feel wronged, why don't you try being human too?

1000687494.english: Wait, just now you were about to say "bastard," but you sanitized it, right?
1000687494.korean: 잠깐, 너 방금 개새끼라고 하려는 거 언어 순화했지?

1000687495.english: How could a Bioroid possibly use such vulgar language toward a human?
1000687495.korean: 바이오로이드가 어떻게 인간님에게 그런 저속한 말을 쓸 수 있겠나?

1000687496.english: I was merely admiring the intuition befitting your position.
1000687496.korean: 자네의 직책에 어울리는 직관력에 감탄했을 뿐이라네.

1000687498.english: Isn't it younger than me? It's just a nickname. A term of endearment.

1000687499.english: Just because you've lived long doesn't mean you get all sly and slyly persistent.

1000687500.english: Heh, the black serpent of the battlefield—that's me.
1000687500.korean: 후후, 전장의 검은 독사가 바로 나일세.

1000687501.english: The cunning snake of the battlefield, you mean.

1000687503.english: I told you it's just a little further, didn't I?
1000687503.korean: 내가 조금만 더 가면 된다고 했지?

1000687506.english: Someone must have performed a rain-making ritual, judging by the cloudy skies.
1000687506.korean: 누가 기우제라도 지냈나보군. 날이 흐린 것을 보면.

1000687507.english: Judging by the context, I don't think that's what it means now.
1000687507.korean: 지금 맥락을 보면 그 뜻이 아닌 거 같은데.

1000687508.english: It is what it means. Now, let's go. We need to meet the squad members—
1000687508.korean: 그 뜻이라네. 자, 가세. 부대원들을 만나러 가야하지 않겠-

1000687510.english: Whew, as expected from this country. Considering we arrived 132 minutes earlier than expected.
1000687510.korean: 휘유. 역시 나라니까. 원래 예상 시간 보다 132분이나 일찍 도착한 것을 보면.

1000687511.english: Is this the Sky Knights base?

1000687512.english: Hey! Did you forget that when landing, it's basic to ensure the landing area is safe?
1000687512.korean: 이보게! 착지할 때는 착지 장소의 안전을 확인하는 게 기본이라는 것을 잊었나?

1000687513.english: Look at this human's face. Isn't it all messed up because of your reckless landing!
1000687513.korean: 보게! 이 인간님의 얼굴을. 자네의 위험한 착지 때문에 다 상하지 않았나!

1000687514.english: My face was like this originally!?

1000687517.english: Why are you apologizing by looking at my face!?

1000687519.english: Why are you trying to fix the situation!?

1000687520.english: I'm just letting you know in advance about the generosity of the Air Squadron Commander.
